lines.st: Always hustling. Busy, busy, Acahuallans.
What are we doing, you ask? Hah, what else can we warriors of Acahualla do?
The greatest weapons, the sturdiest buildings. With the High Priest and Inam Committee's support, we can build even more machines!
Conquest! 'Manure factory!' The Acahualla name will ring across all the lands!

lines.x: 'Manure factory.' What a great word... That's how you say it, right, Yogi?
No, Yota, it's 'manufacturing.' Besides, you should try to get some more rest instead of helping out with work on the... uh... 'Forest Mk. III,' the fully-automatic tree planting and cutting robot.
Actually, since we wrote it off and built a new one last night, this is 'Mk. IV.'
I've been doing great lately, don't worry. I never thought these rocks... Uh, the Originium could be so useful. My eyes are wide open now! Is this new technology? Who brought it here?
Probably Inam.
Ah... It's strong like Gavial, and you can shoot the upper half up in a spiral. It even stays midair for half a minute.
Yeah, it's pretty shocking. Though the High Priest calls that 'disassembly.'
I've been thinking for a while... Look, the lands beyond are so vast, and now we have so much... 'Manufactoring.'
Manufacturing. And the High Priest said before that's not how you use the word.
That doesn't matter. What matters is that we'll have more and more big machines! They'll also get easier to control and cause less accidents, too!
There's much more we can do now, isn't there?!
Like what?
Be ambitious, Brother. Set your sights on all of Sargon. Can you see it now—
Uh, uhh...
—Our statues! As tall as three mountains and as big as two whole lakes!
Ooohh—!
Of course, we need one for the clan leader too... and the High Priest! We can't forget Inam, either...
Finally, we need something special to celebrate all this... A building! Right! A palace! Let's see... with wood? No, it has to be sandstone or some sturdier rock... Let's use Originium! That's it!
We'll pile the Originium up to make a huge building!
Pile it up? I-Into what? Like a temple?
Maybe something simpler. Like a circle... Hm, you can't make a circle with rocks. Let's pile it up layer by layer so it looks like a triangle from all directions!
Oh... Ohh! A triangular tower! Now we just need to give it a name!
Still... there's no point in talking about all this. Gavial said I won't live that long with my disease.
Yota... No! We just have to change the way we think. Let's turn this palace into your burial plot.
That way, forget Acahualla, all of Sargon will remember your name. And I know what to call it, too! 'Yogi Yota Tower!' We'll finish it before you die!
Yogi...!
Don't jinx yourselves like that...
Oh, Tomimi... What are you doing here?
I thought you went straight to Gavial and the clan leader yesterday.
It's been a while since I was back home. I can't just leave like that, can I?
I guess not. So, what are you doing here today? You already stopped by, didn't you?
Actually...

Oh, you're the shortie Inam mentioned. What's the matter? Are you here to complain about our food again? Yeah, I've been thinking the food's been kind of bland lately too. How are we supposed to get by without meat?
No, it's something else. And I have a name. Stitch Canvas. You can't judge me by my height. Are all Archosauria like this?
Haha, no, we're not...
Whatever. You're a bunch of weirdos bickering about tail thickness anyway... I guess it's a cultural difference that I'll have to respect.
Huh? Huh...?
Alright, so what do you need? We're working on a magnificent 'bluepint' for Sargon.
Blueprint.
Uh, right. Blueprint. You hear that, Durin man? This is a huge, glorious plan!
I kind of think... a big, over-the-top tomb isn't a such a great plan... Besides, if everyone gets a huge tomb like that, we won't have enough room in the rainforest.
Stop calling it a tomb. It's for celebration! It's celebratory!

If we build it bigger, we can put both the leader and Gavial in there too, and our descendants will sing the praises of Zumama and 'Gavial's Will' in front of our statues, with their kids all around them.
(I am starting to regret this decision...)
Wha...! B-But...
Right. Right now, we shouldn't—
—In that case, we can't call it the 'Yogi Yota Tower!' It has to have Gavial's name in it! W-We can add my name in there too, while we're at it...!
Uh, no, I mean... Well, Mr. Stitch already talked to Inam about this, but I want everyone to hear him explain it.
This is pretty serious, after all. I sent Gavial a letter too, but I don't know if they'll make it in time...
Serious? What's so serious? I'm trying to plan out the rest of my life with what little time I have left! What could be more serious than that?
*cough*... I'm not here to judge your fantastical ideas, surface dwellers. However...
Just as I said before, if our city actually meets this crisis, the calamity will destroy everything.
And when that happens, it won't matter how amazing your buildings are. They'll be reduced to rubble.
Hey, Zumama.
Gavial? I didn't know we came back at the same time.
How's the situation? Have those Infected settled down?
Who do you think you're talking to? Not just the Infected. Mine security, the villagers nearby, and that old man from Rim Billiton. I took care of all of them.
Old man...? You dealt with our business partner, too?
Yeah, I've got a problem with the way he treats the Infected. I had to have a friendly chat with him, as a doctor. 'From a doctor's perspective and the Gavial way.'
Never mind all that. Have you seen the letter yet?
I haven't been back to the dorms... It's a letter from Tomimi? She went back home to visit her family, didn't she?
Yeah, and she's been keeping in touch with her Acahualla friends through Inam.
Looked like she was going to ask us to go with her, too.
A trip home together... If only Master Closure hadn't given me any work. That would've been nice.
But if that was the whole story, you wouldn't have bothered to come looking for me, right?
Hey now, when did we become strangers?
Never mind... So, what did Inam say? Did Tomimi run into trouble?
Yeah, more accurately, the whole rainforest's in trouble.
What?
Better pack up and get yourself ready. We're in for a big adventure.
T-The whole rainforest will sink into the earth?! The entire land will shift and change?!
That's right.
I could explain the structure of a Durin city, but it's not like you would understand. To put it simply, my homeland is under the ground not far from this rainforest.
We've run into some trouble... and that's why I came up here to seek the surface-dwellers' help.
Now, I didn't actually have very high hopes for you lot to begin with, but I saw what you've been doing. Perhaps you can be of some help.
Well, that does sound like a big problem... but that doesn't have much to do with our tribe, right?
Wait, this guy said if the Durin city's destroyed, it'll cause a chain reaction that will affect the surface... and our Yota Yogi Tower will...
On the flipside, if you help us, we'll return the favor with our Durin technology.
I don't know a lot about your tech level... but I can tell at a glance that your kind can sometimes be outrageously primitive.
Help? From an outsider? Now I'm not so sure we could use any...
Ugh, to me, it's you surface-dwellers who are the outsiders... Never mind.
But Mr. Stitch did point out some serious problems with the 'Forest Mk. III' when he looked at it... although it kinda seems like he got rid of a lot of its functions...
This guy did?!
Yep, that's why Inam figured we should follow Mr. Stitch underground to see what's going on. I-I'm going too, of course, as the clan leader of 'Gavial's Will.'
And while we're gone, it's up to you guys to take care of everyone in Tiacauh.
Well, I've got problem with that... I mean, for the sake of our Yo... No, our 'Great Gold Originium Pearl Triangular Tower of Great Acahualla,' you better get this problem solved!
You used the word 'great' twice...
We will.
But how are you going to get there...? We haven't been outside the forest too much. Are you sure you won't get lost?
Don't you worry!
After all, I am coming along.
Mr. E-Elysium!
You're the Rhodes Islander...

Resilient as I am, even I have to take a vacation to alleviate the stress I've accumulated. Little did I know I'd find myself in another tremendous operation to save the Durins... But never mind. Who am I to miss out on more fun?
Dylan and Blaze were right. It's a bustling place, but you can still relax and wind down a little. A wonderful little spot.
I still have yet to find those famously delicious mushrooms after searching through the woods multiple times, though...
I only hope that Miss Inam will follow through on her promise to help me accomplish the 'seven must-dos when visiting Acahualla.'
Mr. Elysium and Mr. Stitch have already gotten the ball rolling. With the two of them as guides, we won't ever lose our way... or at least that's what they promised.
Haha, perhaps by the time Gavial and Eunectes make it home, we will have already taken care of everything.
She used to be the impatient one... How come I have to wait half an hour in the hallway for her now?
Look at this new gear... I thought Gavial would file a request for this stuff every month, but I guess that wasn't really the case, judging by Master Closure's reaction.
So I guess she's not going back to playing 'wandering medic who left the tribe'?
This is taking way too long! Gavial, what are you doing?!
Don't rush me! You guys put together the most complicated equipment, you know?!
This is what you get when you make an order on such short notice! If you have a problem with that, maybe you should go through proper procedures with Engineering next time.
Ugh—! This sucks! Not even those knights in Kazimierz wear this much armor!
Doesn't matter, if there's anything you can't figure out—
Forget it! This will do! I'm tossing the rest!
Huh?!
Yeah, forget all that junk. Don't need it. The moment I picked up this axe, I figured it all out.
Figured what out...?
That this guy's all I need.
Did you make it?
It was a rush order.
I knew it. Only you could make something this kick-ass. No complaints. From the balance to the weight, all perfect.
But this little ornament...
Lancet-2 told me to pour my feelings into everything I contribute to, whether a patient or equipment.
She said that, huh? Probably learned it from Closure. Well, whatever winds your sprocket.
Alright then, let's go!
...Gavial!
What now, more dawdling?
What... are you going to do? It sounds like the Durins' call for help that Tomimi got might be some kind of crisis their city is facing.
It might be a Catastrophe... It might be war.

The letter says 'fire rained down from the sky and burned our quilts' and 'a giant mushroom sprouted from the ground and blocked the sunlight, preventing the plants from growing,' but it's all too abstract.
No matter what the problem is, we oughta be able to tackle it. I mean, Inam and Elysium are coming too.
Tackle it how?
Didn't you just give me the answer? Check out my getup. I really mean it, Zumama. I haven't been this excited in a while.
So you mean you're using your fists again.
Hah, the usual!
The usual, huh...
Time waits for no one, Zumama.
Don't you want to see with your own eyes what those guys from your tribe have done to the forest?
Hah... I guess so.
Let's get going, then.
That was a real close one, kiddo. You completely forgot the way here, didn't you?
I'm not a kid!
And I'm, uh, just not a hundred-percent certain...
Without a professional guide like me leading the way, how would a kiddo like you have made it through such a dangerous cave? Even I had to think twice before jumping in.
In any case, why don't we take this opportunity to set up camp and get some shut-eye? If you don't know how to pitch a tent, I can help out.
You serious? Is this really the time for that?
The air flows smoothly here, the area is spacious but sheltered from wind and rain, the temperature is pretty agreeable, and it's dark enough to skip the eye mask.
Um... I think Elysium raises some good points! But I'm not too familiar with these field tents...
No problemo, I'll show you what to do. This model's very simple— just unfold it and it's pretty much good to go. Just need to secure a few locks...
Woah, you're right! Very convenient!
When Logistics originally designed this model, they specifically consulted ME, the fieldwork specialist that I am!
I even graciously agreed to let them name the series after me, but I'm not sure why they didn't go through with it.
Well, maybe they felt there was still room for improvement, and that this tent model wasn't worthy of my name? What a shame. They should know I wouldn't nitpick over such minor things!
Tomimi, I've been hearing a lot about what a good time you've had at Rhodes Island, and that's all well and good, but I have to admit, I'm having second thoughts...
Do Rhodes Islanders always pitch tents inside lifts?
Um, I think Elysium is an exception...
I'll say it one more time— this isn't any regular old lift! This is the pride and joy of the Durin people, the Super Convenient Lift-o-Matic #1!
Yes, indeedy. Makes for a great campsite.
Hey!
Enough of that, you two. How long will it take to get to the underground city you're talking about?
Yeah, what's up with that, kiddo? We've been sitting in this lift for almost half an hour already.
It's not like I'm... *sigh*... I can't help but wonder, were you mixed up in all those stories I heard about Gavial back at the tribe?
Of course! Too often am I on the receiving end of that woman's fists!
...I think it might be better if you turned back.
Well, it's not THAT often, but once every three weeks wouldn't be an exaggeration...
Wait! Elysium might just be one of Gavial's regular coworkers, but...
I am Gavial's most cherished partner, the leader of Acahualla's mightiest tribe, 'Gavial's Will!'
There's almost nothing true about that second half.
Haha... I appreciate your generosity and selfless help. I'm sure the city will welcome you with open arms.
(mutters) Hopefully your ugly-ass metal monsters-on-treads will come in handy...
Did you say something, Stitch?
Oh, not at all, I was just saying we're almost there.
Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to Zeruertza.
It's extremely rare for any surface-dweller to get to lay eyes on a Durin city, let alone our beautiful––
Hey, hey, hey! What the heck is THAT?!
A giant waterfall?
It's... a huge water slide!!
No, that's not what's important. Can't you pay some respect to that beautiful, minimalistic place in the distance with a garden...
So this is a Durin city? I thought it would be a bit more, uh... normal?
Mysterious underground palaces and whatnot. That's what the magazines always talk about.
Actually, Zeruertza is rather unique among Durin cities, because it was built by the legendary designer standing before you.
My predecessors came here with the vision to create a city of beauty and harmony, and this magnificence is the product of their philosophy! And minimalism! It's...
Super Spinner Slide!!!
Dylan wouldn't stop going on about how much fun Acahualla was, and I didn't believe him at first, with that wicked grin on his face, but it turned out to be true!
Ooh, ooh! I wanna see, I wanna see! We have to bring Gavial next time!
I wonder if the legendary Durins are interested in doing business.
Is there not a single shred of appreciation for minimalism in those empty heads of yours?! What is that heap of junk in front of you to the greatness of my work?!
Well, whatever... I shouldn't have gotten my hopes up for you people.
Huh, it should be somewhere around here.
Some elders in the tribe mentioned a giant cave beneath Acahualla, but I didn't expect it to be this complex.
Seems like what Tomimi and the others were saying turned out to be true.
If the worst case scenario comes to pass, it won't just be us Tiacauh that are affected. Everyone in this entire region's going to be at risk.
There's no way it's that serious.
High Priest! What are you doing here?
Naturally, I came because I sensed the two of you in need of help. I am the all-knowing High Priest after all.
So, old man, how are we supposed to get inside that legendary underground city?
Don't be in such a rush, children. It's not every day you get to delve deep into an underground cave. Soak in your surroundings.
Take a look at these fluorescent plants. I'll let you in on a little secret— if you grind them into powder and sprinkle them over grilled fin, the flavor simply speaks for itself.
And look at these tiny little fruits on the vines...
Sorry, but we're a bit short on time here!
Alright, I know. Do you remember the safe route coming here? If I recall correctly, it's left, right, then another right, then another left?
And then four left turns in a row after that?
That would take us right back where we started.
Really? Strange.
Let me see if I can pull something else from memory... Ugh, these underground passages wind to and fro. It's hard to keep things straight! Was it right, and then uphill? Or was it downhill?
How about the more dangerous path?
You're just as impetuous as when you were little.
There does happen to be a path that is somewhat dangerous, but saves more time.
See that river heading underground? Jump down.
For real?
The two of you should probably be fine, judging from your statures. But...
You heard the man, Zumama. Get ready, and take a deep breath––
Let's go!
But, I would advise the two of you to just follow me slowly. The thing is... Huh? Where'd they go?
In any case, this is the Durin city of Zeruertza.
I had no idea there was a place like this beneath Acahualla...
Zeruertza has had its roots here since antiquity, but with the exception of a few adventurous idiots, we've generally had little interest in the surface world.
Of course, I am an exception.
You don't much care for stories of adventure?
That's right, I don't care for them at all.
Stop trying to twist my words! I'm not an idiot either!
That's not how I see it!
Ahem... Let me introduce myself once more. I am Stitch Canvas, the most distinguished architectural designer in the city.
As you can see, the buildings around the city that speak most directly to one's heart and soul were designed by none other than myself!
Like that big water slide over there? My respect for you is going through the roof, Mr. Stitch!
Uhh, no, that's not mine.
You must've designed the dome above this underground city, then! It even simulates the feeling of sunlight. Incredible! Good going, kiddo, you're more than meets the eye!
Have you no aesthetic taste? Are you unable to grasp what truly matters about this city?
Uhh... the greenery looks pretty nice?
Alright, we'll need to cultivate some appreciation in you.
Since you'll never get another opportunity like this and I happen to have some time before my next job, I wouldn't mind showing you a place in this city that stands as the quintessence of wisdom.
It is my temporary residence, personally designed to bear the core of my profound philosophy...
Behold––
Inam, look! What kind of store is that?!
A swimsuit shop?
They're so cute!!
Durin clothes won't fit you. Not to mention your thick tail...
P-P-Please don't talk about my tail!
It can't hurt to take a look, right?
Hey, you two. Hey!!
Elysium, we have to bring those two back...
Thanks for your patronage~
Mmh, what an exotic flavor! These grilled mushrooms are as unique as you'd expect!
Kay's culinary tastes are top-class. If the mushrooms of Acahualla are not to be missed, the mushrooms beneath Acahualla are to die for! Care for a bite?
Don't you think you're assimilating way too easily?!
Ugh, my head hurts...
Hang in there, brother! Don't let these little setbacks get the better of you! All our suffering, all our hardship... It all meant something!
The disease is taking me... The time we spent together was pure delight...
There has to be some way, I'm sure of it...
What about that mystical liquid? The miracle juice that supposedly restores anyone to health!
Yeah, that's right... There's still the good stuff. It might even bring back my love of life...
Help us out, kind-hearted passerby. Call out that magical name with us!
You, with the thick tail over there, I'm talking to you!
Eh? Me?
C'mon, dig into your heart, and shout the name with us. Three, two, one!
Old Karl Mead!
Um... Gavial's stew?
Cut!
You said the wrong line, Thick-Tail!
P-Please don't call me that...
Where did Avdotya find such a strange woman...?
Avdotya?
Hold on a sec... You aren't with Old Karl Mead's fan club!
Avdotya's the only sober-looking person in the club!
Wow, they actually use sobriety as their identifying standard...
What now, brother? Should we cancel our yodel-your-heart's-desires-out event?
Not a chance!
You're a Liberi who came here from the surface, aren't you? Fate brought us together today. Come, come. I still have another half-barrel of the good stuff.
Inam, they actually carry around barrels of alcohol with them!
Where did they even get their hands on barrels that big?
This mead's the best stuff in all of Zeruertza! Though, there are always a few know-nothings out there who refuse to admit it.
Wanna give it a shot, Liberi? You won't regret it.
I have to hand it to you guys... You sure are enthusiastic.
All good, it's on us! One look at you, and we can tell you're...
A gentleman of utmost distinction?
Not quite sober. Just the right type to become one of us!
Are you saying I give off far out vibes? Hahaha, that's the first time anyone's said that about me. Never thought I'd be learning something new about myself here.
You can count me in then. Bottoms up!
How about the ladies over here? If you don't mind...
Mm, I'd like a try as well.
J-Just a little bit then!
Wait, come back! Why are you drinking? We still have business to attend to!
Huh? You're uh...
Dear citizen, I know that confronting an important architectural designer such as myself might be a bit intimidating.
But don't be fooled by the fact that these individuals appear idle. They actually have quite a bit of work to do, so if you'll please excuse us...
Are you sure you don't want some, brother? This mead's seriously good stuff.
Me? I'll pass, I don't drink.
As one of the few Durins who needs his brain for problem-solving, I prefer to keep my head clear...
...Ugh.
Why are you looking at me like that? And why are you lifting those barrels up?
Did you hear what he just said? My ears weren't playing tricks on me, right?
This kid says he NEVER drinks? And supposedly to keep his head clear?
We're in trouble, my friends. An alien has infiltrated our city!
Open your eyes, you drunkards! They look more like aliens than anything else!
It's time we let this fella know just how much nonsense he's spewing!
'Harder than ice, longer than winter. More silent than tears, more futile than sighs.'
'Inova still cannot forget the afternoon she departed. The town was like a coughing giant, exhaling rust and spitting blood.'
'She trips and falls, but climbs back to her feet. Her steps know no pause. She doesn't ask for much— she only wants to find a land without dreams, and a few moments to waste.'
'Perhaps a cup of hot tea and a few sips of light liquor. Indulgence is not a sin, at least in her case.'
'What... is she after, and what is she running from?'
'Even she does not know the answers to these questions.'
No need to be shy, Tomimi. It looks great on you.
R-Really?
Ehehe, I loved this swimsuit the moment I laid eyes on it. It's exactly my style!
The tailors here sure are something. They managed to custom-make something to fit your figure so quickly.
I-is there something wrong with my figure? I've been working out a lot harder!
Don't worry. You look super cute.
Phew, that's good then. I don't want people making fun of my tail getting thicker again... Inam, you're not going to pick out a swimsuit?
I'm good. I'm more interested in the buckethead that was helping out the tailor just now.
Durin technology...
That's some crazy good stuff!
I've never had liquor this good before!
Cheers, brother! Let's drink to your health!
A toast to our health! Long live Old Karl Mead!
Hey, Tomimi, about that Elysium guy... Is he actually reliable?

Eh? He's there for us, when the chips are down...
Usually!
Now that you mention it, there are Durin operators at Rhodes Island, but they seem to never talk about where they came from.
Do you think the Durins might be hiding something?
No, I think they just don't care a whole lot. They always seem a lot more concerned about what's on the menu in the canteen.
I never thought we'd get the opportunity to explore an underground Durin city.
Yeah. This might be a great opportunity to spread Gavial's name to the Durin people as well...
You didn't drag Gavial, Zumama, and Kemar with you on vacation this time?

Kemar said she wanted to practice boxing with Blaze. Gavial and Zumama went on a mission and haven't come back yet.
Gavial's been working way too hard recently, and that worries me!
I really wish she wouldn't push herself so much...
I wrote them a letter before I left, telling what you learned from Mr. Stitch, as well as what he told us.
They could've taken the opportunity to go back to Acahualla for a break...
The three of you went and made me Great Chief without a second thought, and it's about time you all came back to see what I've gotten done.
By the way, while you were at Rhodes Island, did anybody find this Elysium guy a little annoying?
Waheeeey, it's a completely different experience!
You, tall guy, I saw you hanging out with those mead boys just now. Listen up, that stuff's for kids!
If you want the REAL good booze, turn your eyes toward our No. 7 Whiskey. Here, cheers.
Cheers!
This aroma... it's like the rainforest after a thunderstorm, the smell of moss in an underground cave!
Oh. Sorry, that's just the wood cup getting a little moldy.
He seems to be enjoying himself.
Oh, Inam, you still haven't told me about how you met Mr. Stitch.
Hm... It's a bit of a long story.
Great Chief, the big things you brought with you last time were really stimulating! Just standing in front of them kept me up for days!
My wife's been nagging me day in and day out about how these things are gonna snatch away my soul or something!
We're talking about those heaps of junk from Columbia, right? Are those metal choppers that big of a deal?

You bet they are! A legendary construction device, those things are, a machine of machines! Have you ever felt the joy of watching those things work right in front of you? My heart's never pounded so hard, not even when I first met my wife!
Huh... Guess I'll have someone stop by to put shock absorbers on your chopper one of these days.
Tomimi's going to be back in a few days, so we've got to get ready to pick her up.
Picking up Tomimi? I can imagine how much work that's going to take.
It's probably about as bad as getting beaten up by a metal chopper.
Who knows how many trinkets and doodads she'll bring back with her? Do you remember how many men we needed to grab all her stuff last time?
At first, I thought it was a whole caravan!
Tomimi's always been like that, ever since she was little. Always hoarding whatever junk she gets her hands on...
Don't you think it's gotten worse since she went to Rhodes Island?
I have no idea why she had to lug back that plate of sheet metal. The one Gavial put a hole through.
I heard she couldn't find a place to put it in her place at Rhodes Island.
Wait, who's that up ahead?
Call me mad, signing up for this kind of abuse. First it was those topsy-turvy caves, now it's this godforsaken place.
But that old man Eartheart drives me up the wall. Just clogs up the doorway and nags you all day... Gah, damn these stupid vines! And that accursed sunlight!
I've always said artificial lighting is much better than this crap!
I'm so thirsty... And tired.
I can't fathom why the Master would leave Zeruertza. Is he a masochist or something?
A little kid?
Who are you calling a kid? You're the kid!
Huh, you've got a pretty funny accent. Who taught you?
I taught myself. Got a problem with that?
Kids ought to mind their manners and be patient with strangers.
What a funny-looking fella. Kind of familiar, though, anyone like this been around Acahualla? Did someone's kid go missing?
Not a thick-tail, or a thin-tail...
Not a Liberi, or a Phidia either. Wait, hold on, could you be...?
A Durin?
What's a Durin? Can you eat it?
I've only read about them in magazines, but apparently Durins are a mysterious race of people who live underground.
You mean a metal crab?
No, you're the metal crab! Wanna fight?!
Oh, I remember now! There was an article in 'Stranger Things' a few years ago about how the Durins were really good at making alcohol! That's something metal crabs can't do.
At best, metal crabs might get drunk off of eating fermented fruits.
They also don't live underground. They just cover themselves with dirt.
That's not what matters!
*sigh*... I knew it. You surface-dwellers are just a bunch of short-sighted egomaniacs who keep running their mouths despite not knowing anything.
You people even make Durins look smart.
Gah, beyond all that though, your aesthetic sensibilities are compelte trash.
Hey, little Durin, I'll have you know— I'm wearing Sargon's chicest styles.

If that's your idea of 'chic,' then it's all the proof I need that everyone up here has trash taste. What is the point in going along with aesthetic merit determined by someone else? You might as well be a lowly pleb.
Great Chief, I think this kid might have a few screws loose. Need me to fix him up?
He's still got a long life ahead of him. Let him be.
I matured a long time ago, thank you very much!
Ugh... Why am I even wasting time talking to these ignoramuses? Farewell.
Oh. There he goes.
Hmm? He stopped again?
Uhh...
Ahem... Uhh, if I may ask a small favor, surface-dwellers...
Can I have some water?
So, the rest is pretty much what you wrote in the letter? He said that a Durin city is in trouble and needs our help?
At least, that's what Stitch said at the time.

Take a good look around us! How could this beautiful city possibly...?
*hic*! How could there possibly be any trouble?
Well, uhh, the drinks here being way too good can be troublesome. Might end up under the table if you're not careful, but that would NEVER happen to me. Cheers!
This is probably that unsociable little rascal's roundabout way of inviting us to see his beautiful home, and I can totally understand that sentiment.
Not everyone's brain is fried like yours, Elysium.
Stitch stayed with us for a month, and one day, he was pestering a group of Archosauria building a train.
He said, uh, 'You folks are good enough! These bits of iron and copper you've soldered together might be able to help me! Want to come down to Zeruertza to have a look?'

So then you–– Brother, mind topping me off? I'll finish yours––
So, you went along with it?
Though Stitch was definitely exaggerating the scope of the crisis, I... was actually curious to see if there's any business to be done with our underground neighbors.
Good idea!

If Acahualla ever wants to escape its savage, brutish state, it'll need every opportunity it can get. I heard the Durins are exceptional at smelting and smithing. If we could do them a favor, we might be able to get a thing or two in return...
You folks won't have to get all excited over a few bits of scrap metal anymore.
Inam... I didn't expect you to be such an upstanding Great Chief! I thought you'd just blow all your money in the city every day.
You brat. Is that how you think of me?
If Acahualla wants to develop, the most important thing it needs is money. Doesn't matter if it's Sargonian coins, Columbian bills, or Victorian pounds. We need them all.
But with Acahualla's current manufacturing output – some shoddy handmade products, a bit of unrefined ore, we barely have enough money for people to live decently, let alone think about getting rich.
Luckily, you don't have to worry about how the city defines 'living decently.'
That said, there's a difference between money and wealth. Sitting on a stack of money doesn't accomplish anything, but once you get it into circulation, then it becomes 'wealth.'
Acahualla is already on the up and up. But to become even better, it needs opportunity.
For example, a technological partnership with the Durins.
Hmm, this is a bit beyond me... All I know is, whenever I run into a problem, I throw some Arts at it! If that doesn't work, I can always whack it with my staff!
That's the 'Gavial's Will' way of life!
Hahaha! Even after working with Rhodes Island for so long, you still can't leave your roots behind, huh?
But, uhh, the best that I can do is some tinkering around the edges. For Acahualla to truly develop, we still need a 'leader.'
But Inam, aren't you already the Great Chief?
A leader is someone who can truly unite the Tiacauh. Am I gonna do that? A little too troublesome for me.
I'd rather go shopping in the city.
I like the way you think! I'm quite fond of shopping myself, but nothing beats the great outdoors, *hic*!
By the way, I haven't seen our little motor-mouthed friend for quite some time. Did he sneak off somewhere?
Seems like it...
I'd assume he went to set something up?
Oh, right, but since we're right in front of the big water slide... It wouldn't hurt to enjoy our vacation first, right?
I, I think the Yanese Operators at Rhodes Island have a saying for this! It goes something like... uhh, 'the host plans, and the guests follow'?
I think that means... Since the host isn't here, we can do whatever we want.
*hic*! Well said! I'll drink again to that pearl of wisdom!
Race you to the foot of the slide, my friends!
Elysium really is getting used to the Durin lifestyle a bit too quickly...
*pant* *pant*... Finally shook off that rabble of drunkards.
That's why I can't stand these soulless Durins! What are these idiots wasting their precious lives on?!
I've only been away for a month, and Zeruertza's already turned into a terrible eyesore. Dammit! I never should've come back!
And as for those stupid surface-dwellers... Gah, as long as they can build me a railway bridge that won't collapse, I suppose I can compromise my standards a bit more.
Stitch? You really came back to us! Word was going around about a teetotaler Durin wandering Zeruertza. I knew it had to be you!
Edge Eartheart... You're never going to leave me alone, are you?
Where have you been all this time? I was running all over looking for you. I wore out my old bones searching around that cave-in!
Your old bones are harder than rock. I, on the other hand, nearly died.
Don't tell me, you... went to the surface?!
What, you got a problem?
I can't believe you did that, Stitch.
You, you're too gutsy for your own good. I never imagined you'd...
Actually manage to do something useful! Hurry, let me see what fancy goodies you brought back!
The network of caves above the lift is way too hard to navigate, and I have no clue where my map went. Nobody's been up there in years!

You remembered to bring me a souvenir, right? Or maybe some exotic ore specimens? Some fermentable surface plants? The latest issue of 'Stranger Things'?! The ones they have at the library are all out-of-date.
No, uhh, how should I put this...?
I brought a few people back.
Huh?
'Thus, she decided...'
'She decided on Old Karl Mead!'
Ugh, that's not good enough. Maybe the product placement should come a bit earlier? Let me revise that a bit...
'Cheaper than cheap. Crazier than crazy!'
'If you need a place to cut loose, have a great time, and, of course, enjoy a sip of some soul-soothing mead—'
'Look no further than Zeruertza and our famed Old Karl Mead!'
Good. Concise, straightforward, and lavish in praise. The client should be satisfied with this. Hopefully.
There's this one proverb Blaze always pulls out. How did it go again––?
The shortest distance between two points is a straight line.
That's the one.
I have a conjecture. You rainforest folk have the terrible misfortune of living in that dump, so you've been compensated with extremely good luck in other areas.
What other explanation is there for why the lot of you haven't gone extinct yet? It doesn't make any sense.
It proves our way of life is good for keeping us alive.
High Priest, what are Durin cities like?
Uhh, I don't remember. A little too boring to talk about.
I spent some time in the underground world a century or so ago and the only thing I can remember is that it was a nasty place chock-full of imbeciles.
Less interesting than watching Originium slugs sleep by the river.
Though, heh, you young kids ought to experience things on your own. You need to have the kind of drive and ambition that I did!
By the way, turn left at the next junction, and DON'T jump down!
How much longer now, High Priest?
Good question. There's no end to the road called life. No matter how far you've come, you can only keep moving forward— plus, there's a geezer like me watching over you.
Oh, if you meant how far we are from the city, if you could drop right through the rocks like I can, you need to go down another five or six hundred meters.
Zumama, how the hell did you put up with this guy up so long? He won't shut up.
He's usually not as noisy around the machines.
Does this chainsaw blade count as a machine?
Harrumph! No manners!
Would you look at that. Worked like a charm.
This is it... We're here!
Uhh, the 'Great Aquapit?'
Let's set aside the Durins' naming sense for now.
This waterpark already looked pretty insane from the distance, but it's even more incredible up-close!
Is this how Durins live all the time?!
Cowabunga!!
When did he change into a swimsuit?
E-Elysium, please be careful!
Woah, that was a close one! Are you alright? Didn't spill your drink, did you?
Well... that's weird. How'd you surface-dwellers end up down here? Never seen you people around before.
And this guy looks about as stupid as a Durin.
Hello, sir. We were invited here by Stitch Canvas.
He said, uh, he wanted us to help him with a little favor!
Stitch Canvas, huh... Would you look at that, he actually came back.
I thought he was going to say goodbye to this city for good, just like that respectable teacher of his.
As much as I'd like to offer you our famous Durin hospitality, welcoming folk that we are, I sadly can't while Stitch is running around causing havoc and ignoring all our ways.
Let me introduce myself first. I am Catch Lightrace, this city's design representative.
No need to be nervous. It'll be over in no time.
What... is the meaning of this?
Hmm? Did the atmosphere just get a little weird all of a sudden? I can't handle all these people trying to toast me at the same time.
Are you guys...?
Trying to pick a fight?! I'm not afraid of you shorties!
You're gonna get a taste of the combat skills Gavial taught me! Phew... Take a sturdy stance, and put your hips into it!
Come at me!
Wh-What is she doing?! Is she gonna clobber us with her stick?
Gyaaaah! Stay away from me!
Hey, hey! What are you people doing?! Stop! Stop, I tell you!!
Huh? I thought we were going to fight?
Why would Durins do such a pointless thing?!
Put your weapons away and come with me!
Ahem! Citizens of Zeruertza, this is this quarter's four hundred and seventeenth referendum.
Following our previous vote on this month's general background music, we decided on the color of our street lights for the week during the last referendum. Now, we must answer a new question.
Since this question isn't particularly important, the individuals here today are enough of a quorum to represent Zeruertza.
Today's question is: Zeruertza has just welcomed new guests. Should we accept them or not?
What guests?
I heard something about a guy who doesn't drink?
Ah, no, it's these ones standing right in front of us.
Cheers, brother! Isn't it crazy that there's a Durin out there who doesn't drink?
Yeah, that's totally crazy. Cheers!
Let's finish chugging this barrel, then we can go play in the water.
Hah, make sure to keep those eyelids peeled! You have to see what us surface-dwellers can do!
Tomimi, sorry if I sound like a broken record, but are you sure everything's alright at Rhodes Island?
Uh, p-probably?
You're always welcome to come back if things aren't working out. Be careful— don't underestimate the amount of damage a bad environment can do to you!
Hurry it up, Catch! We've got a lot of stuff to take care of!
Brother, have you ever heard of a sport called surfing?
Surfing, you say? That might as well be my middle name!
Objection!
This guy can't be trusted!
Ah, yeah. I knew things weren't going to be that simple...
Hey you, drunk Liberi over there!
Inam, they must be talking to you, because I certainly amn't drunk. Not a bit.
I AM talking to you, guy with the stein!
Alright. Whaddya want?
You'd better come out and pick a side: noble mead, or lame-ass whiskey!
We of the Old Karl Mead fan club graciously welcomed you into our fold, and now you're hanging out with those hoity-toity whiskey-sippers? My disappointment is immeasurable!
Not too long ago, we had our arms over each other's shoulders, shouting 'Long Live Mead' together. But I turn around for a second and you go off to play with those uppity kids?
Err, um, I think they're both great...
Oh no you don't! We don't take kindly to fence-sitters around here!
Haha, you're a joke. You can't know what's good and what's bad without a point of reference. Isn't that right, brother?
There's nothing to be afraid of. Speak your mind, and let the words of praise flow for our great whiskey. That is, if you want to stay in Zeruertza––
Hey, stop threatening him, you despicable whiskey-sipper!
Gah. Your mead's baby stuff! Might as well drink it out of a bottle!
Let's throw down in front of the Big Cask tonight then! Walk the walk if you talk the talk!
Hah, you wanna see who can chug more of your baby brew? You have my attention!
Wait, stop right there! The referenda we hold here in the forum are sacred! Talk about drinking somewhere else––
Raise your hand, and let your vote be known. The minority obeys the will of the majority— that is the eternal lodestar of Zeruertza.
Begin the counting of the votes!
If you're raising your hand, then I'm not!
I'm raising my hand whether you like it or not! Piss off!
Just wait 'til we get to the water slide. I'm gonna splash your brains out!
With what, that ridiculous 'technique' of yours?
Alright, not bad. A solemnly conducted vote.
In what world does this count as solemn?
The votes have been counted, and the results are clear.
The people of Zeruertza have made their decision: play to your hearts' content, surface-dwellers.
Cheers!!
By the way, I welcome you as an architect. That gorgeous edifice sitting atop the lake is my pride and joy. You are welcome to play your hearts out beneath it.
You're talking about that 'Great Aquapit' over there?
I didn't come up with the name. It was something we all voted on!
Did they tack more stuff onto the 'Great Aquapit?' It looks even more hideous than the last time I saw it.
That's Catch's masterpiece, you know? You really shouldn't badmouth it in front of him.
He's not worthy of my constructive criticism anyway. Or rather, there's no way to fix it in the first place!
That malformed, twisted structure... Just looking at it makes me want to pass out! How can the people of Zeruertza continue to suffer him as the design representative?
Probably because folks really love playing on the big slide.
If I were calling the shots...
You'd use béton brute to construct... uh, an array of squares.
You still don't know me well enough, Edge. I'd just put a long, narrow strip of concrete there. Minimalism is beauty.
Huh? What about the slide then?
I suppose there wouldn't be any harm in slanting it a little...
Ahem, back to the issue at hand.
There don't seem to be any major issues with the dome right now, but I'm still worried.
Since the earthquake over six months ago, the leak that sprung in the dome has been getting worse and worse. Is that your idea of no major issues?

I'm just telling you what the people of Zeruertza think. The water flow increased? Makes for a more stimulating experience. The temperature control stopped working? Even better, that just means the summer's not over yet!
As for the Originium detection system... Well, no active Originium layers have been detected near Zeruertza for over two decades!
Hmph. A short-sighted bunch.
So, you...
I'm not fixing it. What's worth saving about this cheap, rickety dome? I've said it a million times.
Were you not looking for a solution when you first went up to the surface? Were you not thinking about ways to restore the dome?
Absolutely not. I was just trying to hide from you because you won't shut up.
Now that's a little harsh, isn't it? Just a few months ago, I was voted into the Top 100 List of Zeruertza's Nicest Elders.
Alright, enough of that. I know you're concerned about the state of the dome.
But if Catch can't even solve the issue with the dome's integrity, then forget occupying the design representative's seat, he should get the hell out of Zeruertza.
If we're just talking outcomes, there shouldn't be any safety concerns regarding the dome at this point.

We can leave the temperature control system alone, since our beloved citizens are having a good time anyway. After they get tired of it, maybe they'll raise their hands and decide to turn Zeruertza into a walk-in freezer.

As for that Originium detection system, if you ask me, it was never that reliable to begin with. But since you've been chasing me around everywhere, I assume that's what you're most worried about? I do have a plan for that actually.

It'd be better for us to rebuild the railway bridge that was damaged by the earthquake, re-excavate the collapsed tunnels, and directly install the Originium detection instruments there for more accurate readings.
From the very beginning, the dome was over-engineered to try to do too many things at once.
You'd rather take a massive detour like that than directly work on the dome? Look, even if Finch up and left without saying anything, that's still your work together, master and pupil...
We're done here, okay?
Stop before I actually get mad, Edge.
...Fine.
Right now, the problem of Originium detection takes top priority.
I picked up a few people up on the surface who will probably come in handy. Their hodgepodge of skills is likely enough to repair Zeruertza's railway.
Now, we only need to...
Pardon the interruption, but are those handy people from the surface you're talking about...
Is that one flying through the sky right now?
Huh?
Waheyyyyy!
When you, Zumama, and Kemar first joined Rhodes Island, I didn't think much of it. But now that I've seen this guy...
Be honest with me, Tomimi. Is it really that easy to swindle a paycheck out of your company?
Elysium is actually a skilled guide and a communications specialist...
Well, anyway! Maybe this is who he really is!
Here's the sweet fruit wine you wanted~
Thank you!
Uhh, how should I pay? Or rather, how'd you buy all that other stuff before?
I told the nice tailor granny a few stories about Gavial, and she was so happy she gave me these clothes for free!
As for me... The drinks were covered by my good friends from the fan club, and I put the food on Stitch's tab.
I guess you'll probably survive, no matter where you get tossed.
That's one of the qualities of a professional guide!
Ma'am, go ahead and put our drinks on Mr. Stitch's tab as well. He won't mind.
You got it!
Haha, that guy's stingier than you'd think. Here, let me pay for those drinks.
I am quite proud of this amusement park after all. Naturally, I hope you'll have a great time.
Mr. Lightrace, do you and Mr. Canvas know each other well?
Depends what you mean by 'well.'
When we were young, we ran into each other a lot, since we were competing. We've gone head-to-head over several projects, some of which I won, some of which he won.
The only project neither one of us could secure was this dome above our heads.
We both have ideas for how we'd like to improve Zeruertza's iconic dome, and for a few years, we practically met every day––
During the referendum meetings, we both worked hard to prove that our respective ideas for the dome renovation were better.
But in the end, neither of us managed to win over a majority of Zeruertza's population.
After all, the dome is the legacy of Master Finch, the design representative who came before me.
And Stitch, as his student, is also...
Forget it. There are some things I'm not meant to say. You'd be better off asking him yourself.
Catch, my dear bro, why is someone as young as you talking like a withered old man? 'When we were young?' Come on.
Just follow Elysium's lead. Keep your heart pure, and face life with a smile! Instead of lamenting days bygone, come enjoy a drink with us––
Liberi man, your extra-large barrel of ice-cold beat-the-heat ale is here!
Help yourselves, everyone! Our good friend Catch wants us to have a good time, so how could we possibly disappoint his expectations?
So, my good brother, mind if I order some more? The folks from the fan club aren't paying for other drinks, and there's still so much I want to try.
Are you sure you're not a Durin on stilts?

Are there any other ads I have to write..?
Oh, right.
'How should I describe the feeling of this long-leaf dew rolling down my throat? How ought I praise it without using words so pedestrian?'
'Tis as if I could swallow a cloud, or pluck a string of spring rain's pearls.'
'Tis as if I could feel the falling of winter's last two snowflakes...'
Uhh, they probably have no idea what falling snowflakes are, so maybe I'll go with a more Durin metaphor.
'Two super-frosty fruit-flavored ice cubes fall into a marvelous explosion of flavor––'
Ugh, that hurts––
The High Priest was right, we really shouldn't have jumped down.
It's been a real long time since I've felt like my bones were gonna fall apart. Probably the last time we fought.
Yeah. Now I think I'm starting to hallucinate. About fruit-flavored ice cubes and rum. Think it might be an aftereffect of our fall?
Huh? Why's there a Lupo sleeping here?
Do you think... this might be our fault?
Probably not... right? Uhh, think I should treat her?
Did you bring your medical kit?
Dr. Kal'tsit once said that a true master of medicine can resolve most problems with her bare hands.
Did she now?
Well, I don't remember her exact words, but that's the gist I took from it. Alright, where to start...?
*gasp*!
Oh, she's awake. I haven't even gotten started yet.
Um... Are you alright?
Ugh, my head's still spinning...
Sorry, I think the two of us might've bumped into you earlier. Need me to inspect you?
What are you doing here?
Come again...?
How did you two find this place?
Mind if we ask for directions? Do you know if there's a magical underground city here, or something like that?
You came from the surface.
Weird thing to say. You didn't?
We heard something terrible was about to happen underground, so...
You've finally come, surface-dwellers.
'Naturally, those who come with chainsaw and axe in hand can only spell calamity for this pure garden.'
...... 'The beast of civilization finally sheds all pretense of hiding its razor-sharp fangs, baring its gigantic maw before us...'
I see, that makes sense. A wild beast with a huge mouth and razor-sharp teeth. Don't worry, I'll handle this.
Huh? No, that was a rhetorical flourish...
As long as the thing's less than ten meters wide, I'm confident I can take it down clean. Perfect timing too, since I've been itching to get a good workout!
Mind showing us the way, Miss Lupo? Is it over here?
Maybe it'll be faster if we keep jumping down.
Stop, surface-dwellers! The blood on you will...
Don't worry. I know how to control my strength so it's not going to splatter all over the place.
Zumama, ready for round two?
Right behind you.
Hold on tight, Miss Lupo.
You can trust Gavial. She's an expert when it comes to fighting.
Uh, actually, there's another way... Hey? Hey!!
Thank you, Tomimi. I'll definitely return the favor once we get back to Rhodes Island.
I-I'm not sure I make a very good lookout, but I'll give it my best shot!

Alright, time for a taste!
Hmm, currant mead has a rather strange bite to it, but...
M-Mr. Elysium!
Wh-What's wrong? Are our friends looking for us? You have to tell them I'm not drinking somebody else's liquor––
It's Mr. Stitch!
*huff*... Took me long enough to find you people.
Speaking of which, what's this guy doing here?
Yo, Stitch.
How many years has it been since we saw each other? How awful of you. I bet you haven't even gone to see my Great Aquapit?
Talk about timing though! Now that you're here, should we head down there together? The surfing area's amazing!
I wouldn't mind releasing the flow valve on the water slide while no one's looking, let you experience a true––
Thanks, but no thanks. I've got business to attend to.
Everyone, with me. If you're done enjoying yourselves here, it's time to get to work.
Stitch, why are you always in such a hurry? We've got all the time in the world, so why don't we––
You might have all the time in the world, but I don't. Unlike Design Representative Catch here, full of vim and vigor, I am an Oripathy sufferer with only a few years left.
Oh, sorry, that's not what I meant!
What I was trying to say is we ought to sit down and talk things through. Maybe resolve some of our old misunderstandings...
Listen, Catch.
If recognition is what you're after, you've already won the hearts of Zeruertza's citizens. My opinion doesn't matter.
If you're trying to cement your legacy, you might as well build a super water cannon or something, maybe even paint it red or pink. I won't stop you.
Just don't waste your breath on me. I'll never be able to compliment your... heh, 'architecture,' in good faith.
Let's go, surface-dwellers. I'll tell you all about my plans...
Wait, Stitch, hear me out first––
Let me offer a word of advice: stay away from me.
We have our own paths to follow.
Why don't we talk about that Super Water Cannon you just mentioned, when you have a minute. You've got me interested.
But, before that... Did any of you just hear something?
The merry band of drunkards must be doing something stupid again.
No, that's not it...
Wait, could it be from the dome...?
There's no way. Have some faith in the quality of my engineering, will you?
L-Look at that!
The feeling of freefall.
Wind rushes loudly past my ears.
Some weirdos at Rhodes Island can't seem to get enough of this sensation. Don't understand them at all.
As for me? There's only one reason I'd ever jump from a height like that: to solve a problem.
Fists, staves, or axes. Anything goes.
You solve a problem by finding the bad guy and beating him down. Nice and simple, right?
No matter what I'm facing––
Even if my axe breaks, my staff splits, and my hands can't make a fist anymore.
All that matters is I got it done.
Doesn't matter to me if there's an evil plot, wild beast, mushroom, natural disaster, or some other nasty thing!
I'll crush it!
Hey, Gavial, something doesn't seem quite right. Look down there.
Hmm?
Hey, don't make me repeat myself! Unhand me this instant!
Oh, sorry about that.
What the hell's all this? A waterfall? And a slide?
Yahoo!
Yo, Gavial, what took you so long?
Welcome to the Durins' Great Aquapit!
Huh...?
Waaah, Gavial, it's all my fault!
Tomimi!
So let me get this straight. There's no giant, horrible monster, and no fire or mushrooms raining from the sky?
Elysium called it a 'rhetorical device'...
I'm gonna go beat the shit out of him.
Well, what Tomimi said wasn't altogether false. The Durins are having a crisis of some sort.
Though it's not immediately obvious, that is what Mr. Stitch said.
There's a reason for what I do.
So, what kind of stunt are you uppity surface folks trying to pull here?!
You just broke into Zeruertza brandishing that huge axe, and now you're claiming to be helping the Durins?
What a joke. The Durin people are perfectly capable of solving their own problems. As if they'd ever ask for help from you high-and-mighty surface-dwellers.
What's more, all that filth and nastiness you carry on you is enough to besmirch our little patch of paradise down here!
Uhh, aren't you a Lupo? Aren't you also from the surface?
I've abandoned everything I was once up above. Now, I am only a citizen of Zeruertza.
Therefore, I believe I speak for all the citizens of Zeruertza when I ask you to begone from this city!
If you want to be stubborn about it, I don't mind kicking you out by other means.
Calm down, Avdotya. I brought them here.
Stitch... I didn't know you were back.
I don't know what you're trying to do, but as someone who used to be 'one of them,' I'd urge you to give it up. Relying on the surface-dwellers will only bring ruin to Zeruertza.
Zeruertzans have never seen real greed, a real scheme.
Avdotya, the people already voted to let them stay...
I have no problem overturning that result with another referendum. It all depends on how the Durins see them.
But if push comes to shove, and you surface folk bare the weapons of violence and bloodshed as you always do, then I will hunt you to the bitter end.
Gah, you're not making any sense. This whole thing's just a misunderstanding.
I heard Acahualla's underground neighbors needed help, so I came to lend a hand. That's all. Helping them is helping us.
Oh yes, you showed up at your neighbor's house with a chainsaw. I'm sure you're just trying to help me fix my table leg. I'm sorry, could you use that huge axe to help me cut some fruit?

Don't try to pull a fast one on me. I've seen a kindly Messenger transform into the image of wickedness in the blink of an eye. The pursuit of profit and the lust for conquest is in the nature of all surface-dwellers.
You might be able to deceive my good-natured Durin friends, but you can't trick me. I'm all too familiar with the venom behind your honeyed words.
Uhh, believe it or not, this axe is, uh, just for self-defense.
But if you think we have to solve this with violence, then I'm more than glad to rumble with you.
Hey, you there, with the axe.
You're badass!
Huh? Oh... Thanks?
Avdotya, you're the best!
I've only ever seen you scribbling in your notebooks. I never knew you had this side to you!
What side...?
How far up did you come from? How'd it feel to jump down?
How come nobody's ever thought of that before? Jumping off the top of the waterfall!
Are these two your friends? Look at you, keeping secrets from us. You never told us you had such cool friends!
They might be dangerous. We can't...
Can you ask her to take me up there with her next time?
Avdotya, did you bring anything back from your last trip? Maybe some more of that fruit from last time?
We haven't seen each other for hours. Let's toast to our reunion!
Wait, hold on!
Hey lady, your name is Gavial, right? Can I touch your axe? Is it heavy?
Uhh, it's not too bad?
You got a minute, Gavial? There's a super-secret to having fun in the Great Aquapit! I'm sure you'll give us the biggest, flashiest splash ever!
Here's my address in Zeruertza. If you're not busy, swing by tomorrow for one hell of an ale rager!
Why wait for tomorrow? Let's start today!
I like the sound of that!
Me too! My good sir Catch, bring us another bottle!
What's up with these Durins? Did they get bitten by Elysium or something?
That's a look, Tomimi. Something on your mind?
Gavial will always be Gavial. No matter where she goes, she captures all the hearts and minds.
...*sigh*.
The way I see it, it's just a band of dumbasses attracted to the biggest, loudest dumbass.
Some people just have that kind of magnetism.
So this is 'Gavial the Invincible' that the people of Acahualla keep talking about.
What's she jumping off the waterfall for? Did you plan this?
No... but I suppose trouble comes in threes.
Tomimi, I'm surrounded! Gimme a hand!
Coming!
It's too rowdy here. Let's move somewhere else.
So you're the Durin.
While I was in Acahualla, your name was on everyone's lips, Gavial.
Then tell me, what exactly is this crisis that Zeruertza faces, and how will it affect Acahualla?
That... To be honest, is an extremely complicated question, involving multiple layers including geography, geology, sociology, and more.
Let's start peeling back those layers.
The most difficult part is, we can't discuss this topic without delving a little bit into literature first...
Like Tomimi, I also borrowed a few 'rhetorical' tricks.
In other words, what you told us was an exaggeration?
I wouldn't call it an exaggeration. I just, uh, was looking at things from a more macro-historical level.
As Terra's crust changes over millions of years, there is indeed a high probability that the entire rainforest will sink into the ground...
Can I beat the snot out of this little runt?
By all means.
Wait, wait, hold on! I truly do need your help though, and this matter is critically important to Zeruertza!
Let's look at this from a business standpoint! Surely you've seen it too — Durin technology will definitely come in handy in the rainforest!
The Acahuallans won't have to treasure a few discarded lathes!
Hmm, if you're telling the truth.
I assure you, I'll answer any technical questions the people of Acahualla might have!
(*mutters*) Heh, assuming you can even understand my answers.
What was that?
I-I said, even if you don't understand at first, I'll try to explain things as best as I can.
Let's cut to the chase then. What exactly are you trying to do?
It's very simple. Help me convince the others to repair the railway bridge connecting the city. It was damaged in the last earthquake.
That way, Zeruertza can relocate its Originium detection devices without tossing out this entire damned dome, and I can be free of the old man's constant nagging.
By the way, feel free to blow up a certain oversized slide blocking the railway bridge. That'd be killing two fowlbeasts with one stone. No, three!
Mr. Stitch, why do you have to build an entire railway instead of just repairing the dome? It'd be a shame to blow up the slide!
Everyone voted to leave the hole there and build the Great Aquapit.
Moreover, repairing the dome poses a series of esoteric technical problems which won't make sense to the likes of you!
As such, we have to hold a new referendum to get our plan approved!
I'd rather not. I like the slide myself, and it would be a damned shame to blow it up.
Given how handy you Durins are, why do you need us Acahuallans to help you fix the bridge?
Fine... there's no harm in telling you.
As the designer who proposed the plan to rebuild the railway bridge, I will naturally be put in charge if the proposal passes.
However! Have you seen the blueprint for the railway bridge? I saw it back when I was in school, and it couldn't deviate any more from my ultra-minimalist design philosophy!
That dense wiring! That ugly skeleton frame!
So, I slept through every single one of my railway bridge design classes.
So... you have no idea how to build a railway bridge at all?
Though I hate to admit it, it is my one shortcoming.
That is why I am enlisting the aid of all skilled Acahuallans, subcontracting this important project to you!
So how about it? A flawless solution, wouldn't you say?
Durin society must be incredibly tolerant to allow a little shit like him to live this long...
H-Hey! What do you mean by that?!
Alright, since Zumama is here, she can probably fix up the bridge in no time.
Huh? Where'd she go?
I knew it. It wasn't just my imagination.
...WARNING.
Get over here, little fella!
Bogey. Danger! Initiating evasive maneuvers!
Hey, stay put!

So, if we want to blow up the slide and rebuild the railway, we'll have to convince the business representative, Croque Diamondface; the industry representative, Deculture Silvermint; as well as that Catch guy?
Yes, they hold the most prominent positions, and most Durins will cast their votes depending on their movements.
And why can't you do this yourself?
I see, you must not be very popular around here.
Would it kill you to sugar-coat it a little bit?
Seems like a major pain in the tail. But whatever, I'll treat it like a good deed for Acahualla.
So where can I find them?
Phew, it's finally over. That Gavial is pretty scary.
Seems like you're on the right path though, finally.
Are you happy now, Edge?
To be honest, I'm not sure I'm ready to part with the Great Aquapit.
Hah! Whose fault is it that the damned thing's sitting in the middle of the railway? Otherwise I really wouldn't have been able to find a single person willing to knock that thing down!
Yeah, but I did just have an idea. It's been more than half a year since the dome broke and the waterfall started coming down.
Everyone's been having a great time, but I'm sure they're starting to get tired of it.
Why don't we take this opportunity to build something brand new? I think you were talking to Catch about a super water cannon or something, and it's on everyone's lips now!
And I'm not gonna lie, I'm also a little intrigued...
...*sigh*.

That Archosauria woman! Brutish, uncouth, impetuous, and presumptuous! Can you believe it, Croque? I was having a bit of writer's block, so I went up to the caves to harvest some wild tea and relax...
And someone scooped you up and jumped off the top of the waterfall.
Utterly unthinkable.
Regrettably, I'm afraid that no ordinary Zeruertzan would be able to resist the seduction of that story. If it were put to paper, it would become an overnight sensation.
'You can trust me.' 'Avdotya, hold on tight.' Ahh, it hits all those passionate notes.
Forget it!
It certainly beats your umpteenth story about the snow.
Speaking of which, a couple days ago, a few kids came by to talk to me about those short stories. I thought I was finally getting some readers...
What'd they have to say?
'Really funny. I want to see what happens next.'
I knew it.
Avdotya, they didn't mean any harm. It's just that we Durins have no way of relating to your experiences.
How long did it take for me to finally understand a sliver of your background? Let's not even talk about ordinary Durins.
None of us have seen real snow, let alone the kind that you describe... How do you pronounce those words again? 'Chiaroscuro'? 'Ephemeral'?
It'd be better if those words only existed in literature.

Regardless, I'm happy with my life now. I don't try to control anyone, nobody tries to control me. There are no terror plots brewing behind the scenes, no risk of getting framed, no getting dragged into an assassination.
I write a few ads and design a few signs for our shopping district, and that's enough to keep me fed. What more could I ask for?
I spend my days bathing beneath the sun on my recliner. Sure, it might be an artificial light source, but that's enough for me.
I was hoping these tranquil days would continue forever, until...
Is this the office of the business representative, Croque Diamondface?
Hello, I want to blow up that big slide.
...*sigh*.
Yo, Avdotya, fancy seeing you here!
She doesn't look too good.
Sorry, Croque, I'm afraid I have to excuse myself.
I need to head back and perform some maintenance on my crossbow.
Did I do something to make her mad?
Ahem... Are you the guests who just arrived in Zeruertza? I heard Catch talking about you earlier.
I must say, his assessment of the situation was on the nose.
'A merry bunch more Durin than Durins.'
Evasive maneuvers! Evasive maneuvers!
Hmm, what a weird design. Couldn't be more different from Lancet-2.
Please stay back!
Doesn't seem as smart as her, but it does share some of her same basic reactions. Wonder how this thing works?
Danger level: high! Stay back, stranger.
Take it easy, little fella. My name's Zumama. I'm not going to hurt you.
Defining identity, Zumama.
What's your name?
Attributes: Model R3-E3 assistant robot, number 0429, for the glory of serving the Durin people.
Hi there, 0429. Is there something wrong with your shock absorber system? You're wobbling all over the place.
Self-inspection report: End of service life: approaching. Original components: aging.
I can help. Look, I have a whole set of maintenance tools.
Overhaul... Determining probability of success: low. R3-E3 will be scrapped after service life ends.
From what I've seen, little fella, there's nothing major wrong with you. Probably nothing a few screws can't fix, so don't give up on yourself just yet.
You're a cutie, so I'd like to help you. How about it?
We can be friends.
Friends? Unable to comprehend: friend.
You can help me conduct an overhaul?
Sure can.
But I'll have to open up your shell first.
No. Requesting mission cancellation, requesting mission cancellation. Danger, danger!
Don't run away! It'll be fine!
Did I just see a surface-dweller Phidia chasing after an assistant robot?
Well that's odd. Did I drink too much yesterday?
Speaking of which, Avdotya writes about this kind of stuff, doesn't she? About 'chasing and killing' and whatnot, even if I can't understand half of it.
Well, uh, I guess I'm starting to get why she keeps saying it's not meant to be a comedy.
Care for some tea, Miss Inam?
So, Durins actually do have non-alcoholic drinks.
Everyone needs a change of pace every now and then. Plus, ahem, I AM still at work, after all. I prefer to drink on my own time.
You suggested we talk one-on-one.
I have no doubts about Gavial's skills in a fight, but I don't think her fists will be very helpful here.
After all, we're not so different. You, the business representative of Zeruertza, and I.
I see that you're the type to talk trade. Fine by me, I'm on board.
Zeruertza is just a small Durin settlement. A few years ago, when we were still connected to the other cities, I did have a semblance of a job as the business representative.

But ever since the last earthquake, our sole railroad has been cut off. Zeruertza is fortunate enough to be self-sufficient, the citizens aren't the neediest and there's been little urgency to restore our ties with the outside.
Nowadays, my job is little more than catering to the whims of Zeruertza's populace and pushing out a few entertainment activities.
Throw my weight around with the hawkers near the Great Aquapit, advertise some self-driving cart parts. It's a leisurely life.
I don't know much about Durin philosophy, but does 'not being the neediest' normally mean getting piss drunk and going surfing?
Pretty much.
But if you asked anyone up on the surface...
Pretty much. By our standards, that's not very needy.
So, it doesn't matter what terms Stitch laid out for you. If you want me to go along with your plan to blow up the water slide, you'll have to come up with something that'll impress me.
No business gets done until both sides find the deal amenable.
What can you offer Zeruertza's economy? Acahuallan artifacts aren't enough.
I heard some sharp self-driving cart dealers already used your grand entrance to generate some excitement for their wares, but that's not enough either.
You... understand the way we do things on the surface better than I expected.
Though I haven't been up there surface myself, the library we have here in the city isn't just for show.
I am familiar with your customs.
The Acahuallans dance around a Metal Crab without sleep or rest, and the last one standing becomes the Great Chief. Isn't that right?
Where did you hear that?

I read it in 'Stranger Things.' It's the most popular periodical in Zeruertza. Back in the day, a great adventurer brought a whole big set down from the surface. It's basically our encyclopedia of the surface world.
That's just a lousy tabloid!
Surely you're mistaken... You have no idea how diligently I studied Sargonian to be able to read those books.
No wonder your accent sounds almost native.
Welp, there goes my motivation all of a sudden. I feel like I could probably just bribe you with the last few issues of 'Stranger Things.'
The most recent ones? There are sequels to those books?! Hmm... If you really could procure those, then maybe I could consider it...
I can hook you up with some good stuff.
What is this?
A videotape. Want to take a look?
Found you.
Oh, Avdotya, what a coincidence. Are you here to enjoy the scenery again?
Be frank with me — why did you people come here?
Miss Inam said she'd handle the discussions with Miss Croque, so the rest of us came out here to find Zumama.
That's not what I mean. Why did you surface people come down into our city? What's your real purpose?
I've been guarding the Super Convenient Lift-o-Matic #1 for a long time. Where's the main battalion backing you? Have Sargon's lords ameer finally taken an interest in the wealth underground?
This city doesn't have the loot you want. Leave, unless you want to poison this peaceful little city with bloodshed.
I told you, this is a misunderstanding. Stitch was trying to find someone to help him repair the railway, and tricked us into coming here.
You mean you're his slave labor? And you're okay with that?
Hey.

That guy was acting like the sky was falling at the time, and Tomimi added her own creative touch... *sigh*, I was worried half to death, Zumama was up all night prepping for a tough fight, and we rushed over as fast as we could.
*sniffle*... Sorry, I just wanted Gavial to take some time off...
Well, it worked out... I guess I'll take this opportunity for a little vacation.
So... You people really don't have any ill intent? You weren't ordered by some noble to ransack the town?
Believe me or not. Your call.
You do have a point... Even Sargon wouldn't ask someone as rude as you to serve them.
Rude? I just don't like wasting my breath.
My plan's simple and straightforward. Help out that little runt Stitch, pick up some useful tech for us Tiacauh, and then go home. That's all.
There's still a bunch of patients back at Rhodes Island waiting for me to check on 'em.
You're telling me you're a doctor?
What, do I not look the part? I'm licensed and everything.
If Miss Tomimi here said that she was a doctor, I'd be half-inclined to believe her for a few seconds.
Oh, I can be a doctor too! Gavial taught me anesthesia!
Find the right spot, and then give it a good whack––
Everyone at Rhodes Island calls it, uh, 'Gaviallic kinetic anesthesia.'
For crying out loud, it's a non-conventional modality. Even I rarely use it outside of extremely special circumstances.
For example, when you've just run out of anesthesia and you lost your supply bag because you were beating the bad guys with it, stuff like that.
––I think I'll keep an eye on you people after all.
Don't get any funny ideas. Zeruertza is my haven of peace, and I won't let you destroy it.
Go nuts.
I'll make sure you leave Zeruertza after doing what you said you're here to do.
Well, until then, why don't we check out the shops?
Um, Miss Avdotya... You're from these parts, so would you mind showing us around Zeruertza?
Just a word of warning... If your plan is to case the city and map it, you'd give up on that.
N-No, that's not it! What I mean is...
I absolutely love your outfit! Are there any Durin fashion outlets worth visiting? I... really want to go take a look!
Huh...?
Alright, if that's what you want... There are a few places worth going to.
Are you interested in seeing some Durin jewelry?
I am!!
Stitch––
What are you doing here, Catch?
You used to always lock yourself in your room. What's the occasion?
Look, even I want to catch some rays every now and then. Surely you didn't come all the way out here just to say that?
You're trying to blow up my slide, and you're not even going to let me talk to you?
...Hmph. So you know.
Master Edge told me.
That decrepit old... Are you here to get a confession out of me?
Actually, I'm not opposed to it. This summer has gone on far too long.
And as architects, our best work is always the next project, right?
Gah. I hate that part of you more than anything else.
Which?
You don't have a single serious bone in your body.
Hahaha, I'll take that as the highest compliment!
I'll agree to whatever you want with the slide, on the condition that we talk about the dome.
What's there to talk about?
Let's go take a walk. You should experience the Great Aquapit for yourself before deciding whether to blow it up, no?
Do you have more? Where can I get the next one?!
Pwaaah! That's the good stuff! Inam, want me to pour you some more?
I still haven't finished mine...
C'mon, hurry it up~
It's a shame Team Red Wine and Coffee didn't win the championship! Ahh, they were just one step away from that ship!
You seem to be enjoying yourself.
I must admit, you surface-dwellers lead slightly more interesting lives than I imagined!
What is this a recording of?
The Dossoles Warrior Championship. It's an old video I got from a friend who runs a caravan.
I heard something happened there not too long ago, and I don't know if the event has been held these last few years.
Ahhhh! I want to see more! I want to see the losers make a comeback next year!
Croque, even the most boring form of recreation can become quite stimulating if you spice things up with a bit of competition.
You cheer for the team you support while chugging ale with your friends. I don't think any Durin would be able to turn down this lifestyle.
Plus, alcohol and fast food sales generally double during a competition. How about it? I think it'd fit the Durins like a glove.
Alcohol sells well enough in this city, and I doubt there's any need to hold a competition to stimulate those sales.
That said, hmm... From the perspective of fun and entertainment, there is a certain appeal.
Let me say this to you straight, business representative.
Want to build a super water racetrack?
I feel... New. Complete.
Hahaha, you can stop circling me now, little fella. I'm just as happy to see you good as new.
Zumama: skilled at overhauls.
Zumama, requesting favor. Small favor.
0428, partner: already retired. No longer responding to communication. Requesting assistance.
Shipped along with this machine, built in the same factory. Now, no longer responding.
No longer responding?
Don't worry. Take me to see it.
Zumama, this way.
These components, and this design... Hmm, might be a bit more complicated than routine maintenance.
How does this thing work? And what's this component for? It's nothing like Lancet, and I don't think Closure has ever said anything about these.
If I connect it here... No, I should be more careful. This is your friend after all, right?
Friend? Zumama has described this relationship several times. But I do not understand it.
Repair cannot be performed?
If only I had some information to work from...
Information? The library, preserves information.
Can you take me there?
Assisting in navigation is my glorious mission. Zumama, this way please.
Now that's something you don't see every day.
I've never heard of anything like that in the books. Surface-dwellers are worth looking into.
Elysium, let's have a little race to see who can fly out face-first from the super spinny slide the fastest. How about it?

Hah, you're about to see why they call me Rhodes Island's Legendary Aqua-Man!
Thorns has nothing on me when it comes to this game!
I'll be honest here. I don't have the slightest bit of interest in hanging out with you. I'd rather be eaten by one of the gloompincers in that damned Acahuallan jungle.
Don't be like that, Stitch. I just want to hear your opinion.
Regardless of what the people of Zeruertza think, I know who's truly worth their mettle in this town – and that includes both you and your teacher. You've earned it.
I've never seen anything like Zeruertza's breathtaking dome in any piece of Durin architectural literature. If you could...
If that's what you want to talk to me about, I suggest we stop here before wasting any more of each other's time.
I know you're not so into my Great Aquapit's design. Same goes for the slide, and the fountain. None of them are exactly masterpieces.
