But, Stitch, do you know what is I really want to design? Same as you, the dome right over our heads! We were competing over it for the longest time!
Haha, you've got some nerve saying that to my face. Are you trying to pick a fight?
You and I make good rivals.
Hah! Good rivals? You sure think highly of me. Should we hug it out? Put our arms around each other and weep?
I'm serious, Stitch.
I just want to know. Why did you give up on the dome renovation project a few years ago?
You stopped submitting your designs to the referendums. You just quit. You couldn't have given up trying to win people over to your aesthetic?
Were you really fine with Master Finch's unannounced farewell? He's still your...
Stop talking down to me, Catch!
I just got bored. Tired of it all. Simple as that.
I got fed up with trying to pin all my ambitions on this broken dome, and trying to get the people here to vote for me.
I got fed up with peddling solutions against 'architects with dreams' like you. It's all completely pointless!
Listen up. That piece of junk over our heads, I don't care if it leaks or explodes or even if you vote to tear it down one day––
I don't give a damn anymore. So do whatever the hell you want with it!
But that dome is your teacher's...

I'll say it again.
I!
Don't!
Give!
A!
Damn!
*sigh*... Alright.
Stitch, I wanted to ask you to work with me. The two of us could've come up with a brand-new plan for the dome, which I'm sure the vast majority of people here would've agreed to.
But since you don't care about the dome anymore, I won't push you. I respect your decision.
But, could you give me a moment of your time and offer me some feedback about my ideas for renovating the dome? I'd like your advice as well.
I don't care–– Actually, let's just hear it first.
Really? I appreciate it!
First, I think the whole thing should be more colorful.
Intricate geometric patterns can greatly add to the beauty of the dome, just like stained glass!
The juxtaposition of yellow, green, red, and blue! We'll let the citizens of Zeruertza know a new kind of beauty when they cast their eyes up!
Day or night, the new dome will become the singular focal point of everyone's attention! It'll be the safe harbor everyone comes back to in their search for peace of mind!
A great, dazzling gemstone!
What do you think? From my perspective, it's a brilliant, innovative design. I'm really proud of it. But I'm ready to take your feedback seriously.
Catch.
Had a change of heart already? Then, we'll be partners...
You're not laying a single finger on the dome. I'm going to do everything in my power to stop you.
Didn't you just say you didn't give a damn anymore, and I could do whatever I want?
I thought you just wanted to resurrect that shambling corpse. Or put it to rest permanently. That, I don't care about.
But I will not allow you to desecrate it in this manner!
Huh? This is entirely a product of my respect for the dome.
No. No way in hell. I swear I'm going to beat that idea out of your head!
Maintenance materials, library. Location: Sorted Archives, Area 59. Available bookshelf.
Is this the place?
Affirmative.
Let me see... Hm, is this Durin text? I can't read it at all.
Reading. Book titles: 'The Durin: To the Surface and Back Again' and 'Fine Durin Liquor: 127 Commonly-Seen Brewing Methods.'
Continuing search. New hits: 'Believe It Or Not! Super Crazy Durin Masterplans' 'Research on Cybernetics Information Theory Based on the Influence of Social Behavior'....
Little fella, are you sure they have what we're looking for here?
Probability: 42.377%. Durins rarely organize things.
Hmm, I can read some of the words over here.
The issues of 'Stranger Things' on these three bookshelves are more than ten years old. This row is for Victorian books... and you even have Kazimierz's 'The Red Wine'?
We use them to learn different languages, cultures, and news from the surface.
I see. No wonder why there's hardly any communication barrier between us.
Is this a Durin-published magazine? 'Breaking News! Attack of the Self-Driving Cart!', order one magazine and get a prototype for free... So that's what all those gadgets zipping around outside are?
Croque seriously pushed her cart modification business, and sold those add-on booster packs throughout Zeruertza.
She said, 'everyone likes a bit of randomness,' but I don't really get it.
But why is this in Sargonian?
In order to carry on the fine tradition of 'Stranger Things,' we decided that our own magazine should also be in Sargonian! It's how Durins think.
Are you looking for this book? 'Build Your Own Assistant Robot in 3 Seconds.'
That sounds like it could come in handy. Thank you.
Deculture! 0429 says hello!
You two know each other?
Hello, allow me to introduce myself. I am Deculture Silvermint, the industry representative of Zeruertza.
And also administrator of the assistant robots.
The administrator...
That's right. The assistant robots in Zeruertza are built, supervised, and governed by me.
Model R3-E3 Assistant Robot #0429, what are you doing here?
This unit, submitted a request to Zumama. Overhaul 0428.
0428 has already been retired. Now return to your post.
...Acknowledged. Executing directive.
Zumama, your communication with that assistant robot is pretty unusual, and its responses have been... abnormal.
What's unusual about our communication?
I can't say for sure. Maybe what's unusual is... the communication itself?
You don't talk to the little fellas?
No... They were made just to work.

That's no good. I talk to my Lancet all the time. She's like a big sister to me, letting me lean up against her cold, metal shell, patting me on the shoulder with her robotic arm, singing to me with her beautiful synth...
...Huh?
'Stranger Things' never mentioned anything like that.
Hehe, that was worth the trip!
I wasn't able to find the right outfit, but these little trinkets are awesome! Ores I've never seen before, these strange metal wind chimes, as well as these cute little pendants...
And most importantly! Gavial helped me pick this out...
The only thing that could be better is if Gavial bought a matching set with me!
Is she alright?
Oh, yeah, it's fine. The kid's been like that ever since she was little.
I've read that this obsessive hoarding behavior is called 'Zalak's Disease' in some places. It might be stress-induced or something.
No, no, I'm really happy! These memories are very precious to me!
Okay, if you say so.
So, Avdotya, you have an Ursus name. Is that where you came from originally?
My name is Avdotya Razorpen. A standard Durin name.
But, Avdotya is still Ursine.
What are you getting at?
Calm down, I'm just making small talk.
No... that place is not my home. I just happened to come from there.
What I really don't understand is why you're so paranoid about the surface.
When I was little, I loved spending time with the grammar tutor my parents hired for me. Though she could be a bit dry, she sometimes said really interesting things.
Ursus has a lot of literature describing one's hometown, and my tutor was always entranced by those works. We would analyze their motifs, their composition styles...
Back then, I thought everyone would grow nostalgic for their hometown, and people who traveled far away would eventually find their way back to slumber eternally...

But I later found out that's not true at all. If all the memories you have of your hometown are painful ones, and you find a place more suitable to be your home, then why should you yearn for a place you once were?
It is no different from another stop on a long journey.
Miss Avdotya, what made you decide to leave Ursus?
...I don't want to talk about it.
Things that would make you vomit. My status, my family, my friends, all vanished overnight. I fled with my tail between my legs, quite literally.
From Ursus, to Sargon, to an underground Durin city... Your journey couldn't have been easy.
How could there be a journey with no destination?
I just happened to get lucky. I finally found this place, a small city called Zeruertza.
The Durins here accepted me, and I will repay the debt with even greater kindness.
This is the place I've decided I belong.
And that's why I won't let anything harm them. I don't care if it's out of malice, or out of good intentions gone awry.
That is my warning to you.
Alright...
I was going to ask you to have a drink, just as a show of goodwill. But maybe not––
Wait.
That isn't off the table.
––Ahhh.
Yeah. The way you drink definitely screams Ursus.
Shut up. Durins drink the same way.

To be honest, I'm not much of a psychologist. There are a lot of things I don't understand about the human heart, and I don't really care to understand it. It's already hard enough patching up the body.
But what I can say is, you can never really become a Durin, no matter how much you act like one.
I don't think we know each other well enough to start picking out each others' problems.
I overheard you talking with some of those Durin shopkeepers. The gist I got was that the stories you wrote about snowfields didn't seem to strike the right note with them.
That's right... They have neither snow nor fields underground.
Just to be clear, I don't write like that because I miss being there. It's nothing more than... a plot of land for a story to take place on.
You know that there's a story to be told there. That by itself isn't Durin.
Some scars can't be covered up after all.
But that won't stop me from trying.
Even if you just be yourself, the Durins will still accept you.
What if I want a new self?
Fine.
Sure, the Durins are always happy to accept anyone they meet. This time, it was you people, and me. What about next time?
Since you are a doctor, you must have seen the evil lurking in men's hearts. You've surely seen the depravity to which people will stoop in order to hurt one another.
I won't act like this is a racial issue. Everything on the surface has shaped us into this. All too quick to resort to violence, lusting after power and money, blinded by ambition...
I used to be soaked in it. Now, I want a new self.
Evil is contagious, and can be learned. The people underground have never experienced it, and have no resistance to evil.
You're right, I can't become a Durin. But for the sake of the people of Zeruertza, I am willing to do everything in my power to protect it.
––I will protect this idyllic, unspoiled innocence.
Didn't you say before that the Durins can handle their own problems?
Stop using my own words against me!
Hey, have you heard the bombastic news?
Which?
Croque said the leaders are talking about putting together something new!
Ugh, that plan to turn all the lights in the city green? Can't say I'm a fan.
Not that one! The one about the slide!
I heard Stitch and Catch are having a huge row over the Great Aquapit's refurbishment plans!
The two of them?! How far are they into their debate?
Uhh, I think now they'll use water cannons to blast people into another cannon, and then into another one... something like that?
Absolutely not!
Let's try a different idea, then. We'll use the water cannons to hurl people up to the springboards high above...
Don't you have any ideas that don't involve water cannons?
But it's a brilliant epiphany, and you were the one who inspired me! Well, in that case, do you have any bright ideas, Stitch the amazing architectural designer?
Ugh... I was just going to be the lead architectural designer and dial down Acahuallans' plans a little.

You had the surface-dwellers come to help with the bridge. I have no problem with that. But now we're discussing the thing that will go on top of the bridge and live alongside it, the landmark of Zeruertza!
You don't want to work on the dome with me. Fine, I get how you feel about the dome. Then let's talk about what will be our next landmark after the big water slide.
There's a much simpler and more graceful way to solve this. It could be brimming with symbolism!
It could be more exciting!
It could be more exciting and brimming with symbolism!

Phew, Catch, arguing about this here isn't going to get us anywhere. How about we have a design competition? We'll give each other some time to work on a design and let the citizens of Zeruertza decide.
Of course. Just like all those years ago.
I'm on board too—
Croque? Are you drinking?
I'm fine. I only had a tiny little bit.
You emptied three whole barrels by yourself.
Stitch, I heard your proposal. I'm interested.
And if Catch is up for it, then that's all the better!
Now we just need to find Deculture, and we'll build something new for Zeruertza!
You mean... I should change how I treat them...
Don't you think they're cute?
It's true that they are nowhere close to what you'd call 'human.' I'm no doctor, but even so, I know that humans are way more complex than the machines we have these days.
But that doesn't mean we can't get along with them like friends and family.
Deculture, you're these a 'parent' to these little guys.
Parent, huh...?
They count on you and trust you, just like newly hatched fowlbeasts growing under their mothers' wings.

For the longest time, I've really only seen them in... an indifferent light. All I ever do is what an industrial representative should, the same thing that all the industrial representatives before me did.
We pick our robot managers from the Durins who hang out in the robotics section of the library. We have to master how to create these little things.
It's not actually that complicated. All we have to do is follow the books' instructions and put together the parts that we like... use our imaginations, and they always work without fail.
My teacher spent years trying to figure out the logic behind them, but he came up empty-handed.
From what I heard, my teacher's teacher also researched it, and it's thanks to her that the assistant robots can tell jokes.
Do you know how to make a Durin angry? Nobody knows! Hahaha.
Oh, that's amazing.
As for me, all I'm doing is taking their abilities for granted. You're right. I should try to actually get along with them.
0429, your fellow robot 0428 has been retired. Inspection will not reawaken it.
But that doesn't change that it was one of us. The things that it built will last.
Retirement isn't the end.
Instruction acknowledged. Retirement is not the end. Understood.
Relationship defined with Zumama: friend. Deculture, I would like us to be defined as friends.
Friends. Right, friends. We can get along like that.
0429, did you know? Friends are supposed to help each other.
Before, I used to order you to do everything that I couldn't be bothered to handle myself...
But now I'm thinking that's not how this should be. We should be on equal footing with one another.
From here on out, I will ask you to help me with my tasks, as a friend.
Acknowledged. Help friend.
0429, please—

Send three cases of ale to my place, and bring some snacks while you are at it. After that, please let Master Edge know that I won't be attending today's representative meeting because I'll be sleeping.
That doesn't seem quite right...
Zumama!
Hey, Gavial, you're here!
This is my new friend, Assistant Robot #0429, and its terrible boss, Deculture.
Deculture, meeting invitation, all representatives—
0429, change of plans. As your friend, I'm asking you to do me this favor. Go to that meeting for me.
Help friend. Help friend!
Do it yourself!
Speaking of which, what was our last meeting with everyone in attendance for?
Alcohol was in short supply, so we had a meeting to figure out how to solve the supply chain issues.
Have we ever run low on alcohol?
You skipped that meeting.
Hahaha, this brings me back. The mead fans wanted to make sure the whiskey lovers had nothing to drink, so they bought out all the whiskey in town.
And to get back at the mead fans, the Whiskey Enthusiasts' Association started buying up mead by the lot too.
These drunkards can survive without booze?
Of course not. That's why they started drinking the booze they bought to get at the other side.
I understand both clubs lost some members to the other side around that time, haha!
It's a shame, though. If only they had an appreciation for berry tomato wine.
Catch, you... so that's your poison?
Yeah, what's the matter?
No wonder none of the alcohol fan clubs accepted you...
Well... Never mind, so we're all gathered today to discuss the 'Great Aquapit...'
Tear it down? Yeah, I agree, and the sooner the better.
I'm a yea, too.
I thought you two loved that place! Hell, you go multiple times a week, Croque!
Right, and I'm regretting my patronage. I can't believe I spent so much time in a facility built by a guy who likes berry tomato wine... It's a disgrace.
How negligent I must have been as the industrial representative to participate in a project led by a man like that.
What is that supposed to mean?! Hey, Master Edge, what's with that look?
Haha, berry tomato wine... That's a little...
If you've reached an understanding, then we'll call everyone out to blow up that showy water slide, fix the railroads, and solve all of Master Edge's problems. That's the plan. Any objections?
It's a shame, but an architect can't let his past work tie him down. This is a chance to once again put my talents to good use.
Stitch, you said you'd have a design contest with Catch over Zeruertza's new landmark. You are serious about that, aren't you?
Of course.
Oh? Finally going to amaze us with your designs again?
Well, with all of you here singing my praises, what choice do I have?
Looks like we're in for a fun couple of months. I should stock up on booze.
Seriously, Master Edge, why don't you try some berry tomato wine? It really isn't as bad as it sounds.
Not in a million years.
And that's the end of the voting!
Well, then. Per consensus, this is it for the Great Aquapit, and we will repair the railroad bridge and build a new landmark for Zeruertza at the same time!
Hell yeah! I've been getting tired of the slide anyway!
The new plan better be something totally different! I heard we're getting a roller coaster?
Water coaster!
Looping water coaster!
A catapult looping water coaster! With a super water cannon! And a racetrack for self-driving carts!
Stitch and I will each submit an independent design proposal, and we'll call another meeting to decide which proposal to move forward with.
Stitch? Stitch who lives in the cube?
Hm, I saw a design of his a few years ago... It wasn't much fun, but it had a unique sense of beauty...
Maybe we can come up with some new way to have fun there. I mean, that place is pretty much an open space, and the important thing is what we can do with it!
It kind of feels like Stitch's building would be a good place to lie down and relax—
Just like that super relaxing Gaulish meditation I saw in 'Stranger Things!'
Hm, I think I can work with that.
Phew, looks like everything went off without a hitch? Not that we actually did anything.
But we did see a hard, bitter negotiation play out. Isn't that right, Croque?
Edge! Drink up, old man! What, are you keeping fins in your cup?!
*cough*! I'm trying to take a short break!
We've done a lot, Gavial! We went shopping and bought a lot of stuff and had a decent vacation. Plus, we even helped score a huge project for Acahualla!
And you also made a mess of my feelings.
Oh, don't say that. I thought we made friends.
Yes, it went much more smoothly than I expected.
That just goes to show that you could've done it yourself, you punk.
But, what's next...?
That's a funny look on your face.
It's been a while since I've worked on anything, so I'm feeling a little...
Phew... It's okay. I just have to warm up.
Once I get myself back into the game, surely—
With the results of the referendum, the Great Aquapit will soon complete its historic half-year mission. It will be blown up and turned into construction material once again.
Citizens, I'm afraid this slightly extended summer carnival will soon come to an end.
*sobs*... I'll always remember our beautiful time together forever.
But the super slide is so much fun, you can't get enough of it! I never thought we'd have to say goodbye...
However! In the Great Aquapit's place, we'll have an all-new, prettier, and completely different structure! After all, this is Zeruertza, a city built by the best architectural designers!
Stitch Canvas and Catch Lightrace are two awe-inspiring, up-and-coming architectural designers. Over the next few months, the two of them will submit their design proposals...
Imagine the show showdown they have in store for us.
Hehe, this brings me back to the dome design contest they had a few years ago, when they just made their debuts!
The duel ended all those years ago without a winner, but who will win the right to design Zeruertza's next landmark? Let us—
Let me get a sip of water first.
Croque, I've never seen you so enthusiastic. Have you been drinking?
No. Not yet, at least.
Now this isn't like you at all.
I was watching videos of a competition on the surface that I got from Inam, and it seems they have a role called 'the MC'.
I thought I might dip into a new career path.
Anyhow! Although the architects' designs are yet to come, my fellow citizens, the tail end of our summer is going to be a fleeting one!
Before the slide is blown up, let us enjoy it all we can!
Ohhhh!
Gavial!
Tomimi, watch out! Slow down!
I was so, so jealous when I heard you guys went to the waterfall in Acahualla!
Hehe, and this time I get to join in on the fun!
You're such a kid.
Hm? What's wrong? You're usually so careful to hide your tail. Let's see... Did it get thicker again?
Eh? Nope, not even a little! I checked with a measuring tape before I got here! It's been months since I got any thicker!
Oh? Sounds like you're really hung up on it.
Waaaahhh... Eat my water blast!
Heh, you little runt! It's on!
They're more like Durins than you are.
How would you know what it means to be 'Durin', Lupo?
You seem a little nervous. Are you worried about your contest with Catch?
Worried? Over him? Don't make me laugh. I haven't fallen so low. It's just laypeople tend to like his proposals more.
Mine get their fair share of fans, too. Perhaps I should be bringing something different to the table. That's how I'll win.
Perhaps I should just... *sigh*! I should probably take my mind off of that right now.
To tell the truth... if we don't care about aesthetics and just look at how fun it is, this 'Great Aquapit' isn't so bad.
Oh, so you enjoy these things, too?
Every now and then.
Stitch, you're here!
Want to try out the slide with the downstream speed set to max? It wears the equipment out, so we don't usually get to do it.
I just tried it a couple times with Elysium. It was amazingly fast!
It does hurt a little when you hit the water, though. Still, wanna try?
No, thanks. I'm not one bit interested in fun and games like...
Whooo! Bombs away!
Catch, my brother! This impact force is on another level! Did you see how high that splash went?!
Maybe we should try something different. Say... see which of us can land with the tiniest splash?
Keep the splashes small? Good idea! Sounds like it won't hurt nearly as bad.
My sand castle...
(Hey, uh, Catch, did we perhaps... screw up?)
(Uh, even though this is Stitch we're talking about, he can't possibly be mad over a sand castle, right?)
(He seems rather angry to me, though.)
(In that case, we should probably...)
Hey, where are you two going? You're just going to run? Get back here!
Yep, you did very well getting everyone going.
I have your video tapes to thank. That's where I picked it up.

Next, we can hold a series of contests to keep the enthusiasm that goes with this 'championship' contest going, then we'll use Catch and Stitch's design contest in a few months to pump everyone up to the max.
We could print some paper slips for the audience to use to show their support for one side, with prizes given to those who pick the right side.
We call those lottery tickets. You're really good at picking things up. Up on the surface, there are merchants who line their pockets by selling them.
We Durins don't much care for money in that way... Let's have the losing side pay for the winners' drinks for a month!
Hm, we could make it two months to make things more exciting, too. We'll have to decide with a referendum.
Later on, perhaps we could issue some more lottery tickets covering how many months' worth of alcohol are owed in the end.
I'm starting to worry about the Zeruertzans' future.
Hm, I'm not used to feeling unhelpful.
What's wrong? Do you have to beat something up to satisfy yourself?
Not really.
Still, if this goes well, Acahualla will get its hands on some Durin. Would've been a totally pointless trip otherwise.
Gavial may have left home a long time ago, but she actually really cares about Acahualla!
Yeah, I've been to so many different places, but the rainforest is the one place where I feel most at home.
Are you going to come back, then?
Not planning on it. Not yet, at least. I still have lots of patients to take care of back at Rhodes Island.
There are plenty of Tiacauh who want you to go back. You could think about becoming their lord ameer, even.
Yeah, and they want bananas to sprout straight out of the soil, too. Can't always get what you want.
Gavial, you would be the most fitting lord ameer for Acahualla. You are the only one who can rein in each and every Tiacauh.
Everyone is awaiting your return.
And if you simply cannot do it, then it's ultimately likely... that a lord ameer from the Court of Sargon will come to govern Acahualla instead.
And when that happens, Acahualla will no longer be the free land you knew.
But don't we already have you for our Great Chief?
There are some things I can't do anything about.
I can come up with ideas to help get us in better shape, but I'm not the one who can really bring the Tiacauh together.
It's not like I'm running away from responsibility here, Inam.
It's just... I haven't thought through how to actually do it.
You know how to think? Color me surprised.
Hey, Zumama, you picking a fight?!
What I'm saying is, you usually do stuff without thinking.
And that's fine when it's just me, but this is about all those Tiacauh...
All good things must come to an end. Isn't that right? So this giant slide will be torn down for something new.
I actually didn't catch much of what you were talking about. All I want to say is, my friends, let me get a picture of all of you together while it's still standing.
Haha, alright, let's toss all this complicated stuff aside for now.
Right now, we gotta have fun!
Tomimi, you gimme a smile, too!
Looks like you've mastered the Durin philosophy.
Well, it's not often we get a vacation. As if I'm gonna waste it!
Well said! Here's to you! How about some berry tomato wine? It's on me.
Nah, I'll have to pass on that.

lines.st: The demolition explosives have already been set, and the assistant robots have made all the preparations.
Soon, the big water slide that has brought everyone so much joy will be gone without a trace.
But summer must come to an end, and there will no doubt be greater fun in the future.
That's what all the Zeruertzans believe.
Five, four, three, two, one.
The explosion isn't nearly as loud as anyone expects. The slide simply slowly disintegrates.
Or perhaps, a little more poetically, it melts into the water.

lines.x: My, what a graceful way to disappear.
Here I thought you would pass out from all that drink and miss the demolition.
Haha, I know how to have a good time.
Though, at times like these, I generally keep myself clearheaded.
That's the most basic respect I have for things as they fade away.
Whoa, were you actually a secret genius? That would be such a cheap cliche, even in the Zeruertzan library.
Oh, you little rascal!
Stitch.
Catch...
It's time we got serious. Let's both do our best, fair and square.
Shake hands if you agree?
Alright... Out of the most basic respect for another Zeruertzan.
Say, you two actually get along pretty well, don't you?
What, are you blind?! Where'd you get that idea?
Now, things are going to get real from here on out!
Has Croque been drinking...?
The warm-up of warm-ups, the prelude to preludes! The first season of the Zeruertza Competition Series!
*Drumroll*—Ta-dah! A swimming contest!
How about it?
Without further ado, let's begin the first joint Zeruertza-Acahualla-Rhodes Island swimming competition!
I'm Croque Diamondface, Zeruertza's business representative. This broadcast is brought to you by Deculture Silvermint's newest high-definition camera.
First, let's take a look at the course.

The race will take place on an underground lake that stretches from here, all the way to the tunnel.
Here are the rules:
Anything goes, and whoever gets to the other side of the lake first is the winner!
The winner will get an unlimited supply of my special craft ale for one month!
The Rhodes Island representative is—
The Wind of Iberia, Rhodes Island's sexiest man, Elysium!
What's up, Durins?
Was that introduction why he pulled Croque to the side to talk to her?
The Acahualla representative is—
Gavial!
Go for it, Gavial!
Hey everybody!
And we have a surprise for the Zeruertza representative—
Master Edge, our environment and climate representative!
How did a tunnel survey turn into a swimming competition?
It's a rare opportunity, isn't it?
We're still waiting on the equipment, after all.
Plus, it's not easy to go back and forth with the railway out of operation. If we're going over anyway, we might as well do something fun with it.
Sigh... have it your way.
Aren't you coming?
Not interested. I would rather check up on Tomimi and Deculture's progress.
What a boring Ursus woman.
I'm not Ursus!
And how did you convince Master Edge?
I volunteered, Avdotya.
But...
I just wanted you to help me out while you're having fun.
Now that you've helped me, I certainly won't object to this little event.
We have plenty who like to play in the water, but not a single one who's actually a good swimmer.
This is about the pride of Zeruertza, after all, so I've got to put this sack of old bones on the line.
Don't sweat it, Master. It's all friendly competition.
You say that, but I can't have people talking about how Zeruertza lost to a bunch of surface-dwellers!
Avdotya said she's not interested. She didn't say she wouldn't do it.
Durins aren't a pushy people. If she doesn't want to join, I won't force her.
Plus, Avdotya isn't a native of Zeruertza. Makes sense she wouldn't want to represent us.
Looks like it's up to me then.
...Alright, alright, I'll compete on behalf of the Durins!
Happy now?
Really?
How can I not, after what you said?
That's more like it!
Croque!
Are you really going to participate, Avdotya?
Is there a problem?
(Whisper) Unlimited supply of craft ale for one month. It will be a real hit for me if she wins this.
Well, it's just friendly competition. No need to be too serious about it.
I'll win this! For Zeruertza, and for the ale!
Er, alright, good luck.
Excuse the interruption. We have an announcement to make.
Avdotya will be subbing in for Master Edge as the representative of Zeruertza.
Let's give her a round of applause!
You still have to come with us across the river, though, Master Edge.
How about you ride on my back during the competition? I'll take you across.
Oh?
Was that intended to be an insult, Gavial?
Hm? No.
I just don't think I'll lose.
Speaking of which, you should probably get changed if you're gonna race.
Since you're carrying extra weight, these clothes will do just fine.
Oh?
Just because I don't exercise often doesn't mean I'm out of shape.
I'm not so weak that I'll lose to a Sargon.
Ha. There it is, that's the face. Haven't seen you make a better one yet.
Dúra.
Contestants, please take your positions.
They're calling us.
I have ears.
Maybe this isn't the best time to interrupt, but did they forget that I'm competing as well?
Whatever. They'll find out who has the real skills when the action starts.
On your marks.
Ready.
Hang on tight, Master Edge. Things could get a little rough once the race starts.
Don't underestimate the wisdom of age.
Get—
Damn it, I got provoked into letting my Ursine slip.
I'll beat you, Gavial.
Set—
Oh, come to think of it, I should have asked Croque to add the title 'Lone Ranger' to my name.
I'll remember for next time.
It just occurred to me... don't I have to follow them if I want to get a good angle with the camera?

But I don't have a boat, and I can't exactly hold a camera while swimming. What do I do?
Ah well, never mind.
Go!
At Croque's command, the three contestants — plus one passenger — leap into the water.
The race begins.
Gavial has taken the lead.
Ever since she came to Zeruertza, Gavial has wowed us with her superb athleticism.
She's gotten ahead of the pack, even with Master Edge on her back!
They're having a little too much fun.
Why don't you join them, Stitch Canvas?
Deculture?! Why are you here? Aren't you working on repairing the railway?
No problem, I've put the bots to work there.
Keep your distance. I have Oripathy.
Oripathy doesn't spread so easily, does it?
Plus, I like the peace and quiet of your place.
Well, there's a surprise. Gavial is still in the lead, as expected.
But how many of us would have expected Avdotya to be hot on her tail?
Who could have guessed that the elegant lady of Zeruertza has such power within her?
She's keeping pace with Gavial, who hasn't been able to shake her.
Oops, I can't see. Can someone give me a shoulder to stand on?
There we go. Thanks.
I've read in 'Stranger Things' that the people of Ursus are a hardy folk, able to live on nothing but ice for three months. I still didn't expect Avdotya to really keep pace with Gavial...
You sure are interested in what's going on there. Why don't you go over and have a look?
Not interested. It's a waste of time.
Really? You've hardly touched your sketch since the race started.
What do you want, Deculture?
I'm just curious, Stitch. What's troubling you?
Because you felt like you deceived Gavial and her friends?
Things don't have to be so complicated. Everything will fall into place if you agree to fix the dome.
You don't want to fix the dome, so you're using the vote results as an excuse not to do it. Instead, you made the proposal to demolish the slide and repair the railway.
And I'm not wrong! Blowing up the slide and fixing the railway will allow us to detect the spread of active Originium!
I didn't say you were wrong. While machine surveying of Originium has fallen out of fashion, the equipment we have is still usable.
Master Edge's problems will be solved once we build a boat and set things up.
He won't be pestering you about the dome anymore. You can continue your design work in this hut of yours, competing with Catch, and everybody lives happily ever after.
Why are you anxious?
What are you anxious about?
We're at the halfway point, 300m point.
What's that? It's 400m already? Alright.
Anyway, the order hasn't changed since the start of the race, with Gavial in the lead, Avdotya in second place, and Elysium in third.
Al-though Elysium has not overtaken the others, his form is a thing of beauty!
Is he staying in third place on purpose?
I just feel uneasy.
Like I've dumped everything on them.
I agreed to compete with Catch on a design for a new Zeruertzan landmark... but how can I convince the people? I have no confidence there.
You'll get along so much better with Catch if you're being honest about your feelings with him.
...It won't happen.
I'll never like him, and he'll never like me.
Hm... well, I'm just looking for a place to take a break. It's none of my business if you want to keep staring at a blank piece of paper.
But if you want to check things out—
Deculture, Deculture. The first boat for the railway project is complete and ready by the lakeside.
The excavation and survey equipment that Master Edge asked for. You can help me deliver it.
Thanks.
Deculture, Deculture, taking care of Stitch.
I'm not the only one taking care of him.
Well, I can take a nap now that he's gone.
I'll leave the rest to you and the surface-dwellers.
Do not worry. Zumama, our friend, will help. Do not worry.
You like her that much?
Zumama cares for us. We like Zumama.
What a strange surface-dweller.
We're here by the lake to get a better view!
Hey, is that Stitch the architect over there?
Wait, stop fidgeting! I'll lose my footing!
Whoa!!!
The Durins have made a Durin tower for Croque, so she can see the race better.
It is not easy to keep up a tower consisting of dozens of Durins, however, and it begins to lean at Croque's movement, eventually—
Ouch, my butt...
The robot said the boat is parked around here.
This is the equipment that Master Edge asked for?
I'll load Gavial's equipment onto it as well, and ask someone to bring the boat across.
Hff... phew...
This is the boat?
Stitch? I think so.
Off I go then.
Huh?
Oh, he just got onto the boat that the robots brought to the lakeside, and he's sailing across the lake!
Did he come to join the competition?
Come to think of it, the rules don't say you can't use vehicles!
Zumama, boat assembly: correct?
It looks structurally sound, but there are a few things we can tweak. For example—
There, that should make it more solid.
Zumama, Zumama: amazing.
Just the product of experience.
There you are, Zumama.
Inam? What are you doing here?
This survey equipment has to get across, right? I came to help since I didn't have anything else to do.
Thanks.
Why are you thanking me? Looks like you're actually becoming one of these robots.
Yes, I'm glad to make so many new friends.
Zumama, Zumama: friend!
And I've been trying to find a date for Lancet-2.
Lancet-2 is the robot that came with Rhodes Island, right?
Yes, she's my big sister now.
I'm not skilled enough to make a robot as smart as Lancet-2, so I'm trying to find one in this group.
And...?
They're very nice, but not really her type.
She likes intellectuals, like the Doctor. It's a bit of a headache.
Never mind, you're not the only weirdo in Tiacauh.
Hey everybody, this is Croque, and I've found a safer vantage point to continue the broadcast from.
How far until goal? 300m? Ah, forget it.
What's really important is that the architect Stitch Canvas, the only student of Master Finch, has found a boat and jumped into the race!
Oh? Looks like he's talking to us. Let's zoom in and see what he's saying...
'I'm gonna win,' I think. Yup, I'm sure that's it.
And he just raised his middle finger to show his motivation. Looks like there's a fiery heart beneath this architect's cold surface!
He may have gotten a late start, but that vehicle is definitely a huge advantage.
He's closing the distance as we speak!
Things are pretty lively over there.
I wanted to say that this is what happens with Gavial around, but she's not the only one who's let her hair down.
You're not used to it?
You have to get used to it, living in Acahualla.
You get so used to it I feel weird if there's no one arguing and making noise around me.
You're a proper Tiacauh now.
Of course I am.
Speaking of which, Zumama, if Gavial didn't come back the year before last, you were planning to become the Great Chief of Acahualla, weren't you?
Becoming Great Chief wasn't the real goal; it was a way for me to prove myself.
I wanted to build the most powerful machine ever.
I knew I shouldn't have placed any expectations on you.
Are you thinking about being lord ameer?
Good to see that you and Tomimi have learned something about politics, unlike Gavial.
I don't know how she's survived on the outside all these years.
Some things we deal with as a matter of course just don't factor in for Gavial. Like adapting to your environment.
Haha, that's true.
So you want her to become lord ameer? Are you sure she's suitable?
Pretty sure you could pick somebody off any street in Sargon and get a better lord ameer than her.
Agreed.
But if we're choosing a Tiacauh to lead Acahualla, there's no one more suitable than her.
Are you speaking as a Tiacauh, or as a Sargon Messenger?
We're entering the final stretch of the race!
Gavial remains in first place, but her lead continues to shrink.
It's not Avdotya who's closing the distance, though!
Despite her valiant effort, her choice of clothing has become dead weight.
Literally, as the weight of her clothes is slowing her down!
Meanwhile, Elysium is rapidly closing in!
Conserving his strength in the first half, Elysium now shows off his true ability.
He has passed Avdotya and is almost neck-and-neck with Gavial!
However—
Neither of them are aware of the true threat approaching!
I'm impressed with Elysium. I never thought he could catch up to Gavial.
His attitude is a mask. You can't get complacent.
Yes, I noticed. He doesn't want people to know how good he is.
Don't change the subject.
Alright, looks like you've learned a thing or two at Rhodes Island.
Not on purpose, but Master Closure makes me go to meetings about stuff besides technology, so I've heard a bunch of things.
Looks like she has high hopes for you.
As for your question...
I've lived in Acahualla for a long time.
Compared to city life, I prefer to stay in the jungles, watching you people fight over stupid things.
Maybe it's hard to believe, but for me, I'm a Tiacauh a Sargon Messenger at the same time. I don't have to pick.
Or I guess I could say, my Sargon perspective tells me that Acahualla won't stay unexplored forever. That's why I hope you'll seize the opportunity.
I believe you.
We're headed towards a climax.
Exercising his explosive potential, Elysium has almost caught up to Gavial.
Avdotya's not out of this race yet, though, and she's still keeping pace with the two in front of her.
No need to push it, Avdotya!
But now all three of them have noticed the threat approaching from behind—Stitch, riding in his boat!
The wonders of machinery! He has caught up to the three in front of him in a very short period of time!
Only a hundred meters to the goal!
It's like we're at the starting line again. Three swimmers and a boat, racing their way into the final stretch!
But, Inam.
I won't help you convince her.
Gavial should not sit in that chair.
You'll get the same answer if you ask Tomimi.
Why?
Figure it out for yourself. I'm busy.
...Fine.
If you have too much free time, you can help me build this boat while you think about it.
You really count every last coin, don't you?
Eighty meters!
Avdotya got caught in the boat's wake. She's coughing and she has to stop! Yes!
Er, I mean, what a shame.
Fifty meters!
Elysium tries to board the boat, but took an accidental hit from Master Edge, who is angry at Stitch, and falls back into the water!
There are bubbles coming to the surface. Is he okay?

Thirty meters!
Master Edge leaps from Gavial's back onto the Ale Keg, and hits Stitch on the head!
Ouch! That must have hurt!
By the way, Ale Keg is the name that I just gave to this boat, following in the footsteps of my other masterpiece, 'The Great Aquapit'!
Looks like the winner will be Ga—
Wait, not quite!
The fight between Master Edge and Stitch has destroyed the boat's instruments!
There's an ominous rumble coming from the Ale Keg!
She dashes ahead like an arrow!
Not even Gavial can catch the out-of-control boat!
The boat is the first to arrive at the goal!
The winner of this swimming contest is—
The Ale Keg!
...What?
Wait, it's not over yet!
The Ale Keg isn't satisfied with simply crossing the finish line!
She rushes ashore and charges toward the cave-in!
Boom!!!
Master Edge and Stitch are thrown from the boat!
The Ale Keg has put a huge hole in the rock blocking the tunnel exit!
Poor Ale Keg, never to taste the breakfast of champions!
A moment of silence for her!
What on earth are these people doing?
Stitch opens his eyes, and sees two familiar figures.
Avdotya, I've got your fresh change of clothes here.
Thank you.
Also, Croque, drink a little less in future, to be on the safe side.
Ahahah, sorry, sorry. All's well that ends well, though, wouldn't you say?
No, I still don't see anything 'well' about this...
In the meantime, I suppose I'll find somewhere to change.
With that, the two of them notice Stitch has come to.
*sigh* Good morning, Stitch.
What happened to me?
Do you still remember how you fainted?
I was steering the boat, and then old Edge came running over, and then... oof, my head...
And then the boat went out of control, crashed against the shore, and flung the two of you out.
And how about Master Edge?
He woke up before you, and he's taken Elysium with him to survey for now.
Wait, the gear on the boat!
The equipment's all essentially wrecked. As for the survey gear... we had Deculture make that on the fly.
The cutting equipment won't be necessary, though.
Why?
You didn't notice the terribly loud noise ever since you woke up?
Avdotya flashes a begrudging glance at a place too not far off.
And with her prompting, it finally hits Stitch that the persistent racket isn't just nature. Is that a mixture of a chainsaw's roar and collapsing rock?
Gavial, what in your right mind are you doing...?
Looks obvious to me. I'm sorting out this cliff face.
The boat did put a considerable dent in the caved-in area, but clearing a passage was never going to be that simple.
And with our cutting equipment down, I thought we'd be waiting for a while, but it turns out—
Well, seeing is believing.
HAHA! Forget rail repairs and surveys, any bozo can tell THIS is my strong suit!
Gazing all around, Stitch sees numerous fragments of extremely orderly cut rock, as well as a tunnel wide enough to fit two side by side.
The fine work of Gavial, without a doubt.
Which is to say, deprived of all the cutting equipment, Gavial opened a way through to the tunnel all by her singular self.
Stitch can't help but begin to suspect that the rumors he heard from the Acahuallan tribes, all those legends of the invincible Gavial, were at least partly founded in reality.
Oh, hey, Elysium. How come you're back so soon?
What happened? Where's Master Edge?
Things have gone a little south, Gavial.
Huh? You mean some giant Originium slug showed up, or what? I heard Provence saw something like that in Siesta.
If it was just some creature, I guarantee I'd be happy to wisecrack with you right now.
Follow me.
After having drilled through the cave-in, everyone enters the tunnel itself.
The space inside is more of a cave than a tunnel in reality, both vast and profoundly deep.
Nevertheless, Elysium is more than up to the task, and everyone proceeds through smoothly with him leading the way.
So smoothly, in fact, that it piques their curiosities that he could grasp the tunnel's structure in such a short time; there's even suspicion he's been this way before.
However, that idea, more a joke than anything, pales in comparison to the hesitance at the sight that greets them where Elysium comes to a halt.
It is a vein of Originium, exposed on the surface of the strata.
Moreover, anyone with some knowledge of Originium could easily tell that this vein has been active for a very, very long time.
And activity means—
Explosion.
Or put less kindly—
Disaster.
How can this be?!
We'd detected the presence of Originium veins in this direction back in the city's founding times, but they should've been much further out—
Is there any chance the earthquake pulled them here? I heard about a few small aftershocks in the wake.
The suddenness of this vein definitely matches, after all.
It's not impossible...
Right, that's it! Chances are that earthquake shifted it right over here!
And what's more, it gave it that push, got its activity slowly warming up.
And because the dome's Originium sensor facilities have been down all this time, that's why we only just now found this out—
Stitch, calm now. This isn't your fault.
We made this decision as the collective inhabitants of Zeruertza.
Thing's just...
We haven't gone even a half kilometer from outside, I'd wager. If a full vein of active Originium explodes only that deep in...
Then Zeruertza's out of luck.
Attention all residents of Zeruertza, we interrupt this broadcast with an urgent notice.
Following the conclusion of our swimming competition prior, Master Edge has taken the initiative in leading a selection of other contestants through the cave-in to survey the tunnel.
The results of this survey are not optimistic.
Master Edge has discovered an Originium vein in the tunnel, located approximately one kilometer from the city.
We tentatively speculate this Originium vein's existence was known at the time of Zeruertza's founding, in a stratum approximately three kilometers out.
However, tectonic shift originating in the earthquake half a year ago caused this vein to warp and extend closer to our city, and kickstarted its activation in the process.
Currently, Master Edge is leading experts to urgently evaluate this Originium vein's level of activity, and thus determine at what point in the future it will explode.
We will provide further updates once accurate conclusions can be drawn.
As of now, we ask all residents to be prepared to evacuate.
Huh? An active Originium vein's going to explode? Where's this coming from?
Judging by the broadcast, we never picked it up after the earthquake wrecked the dome, since the Originium sensor functions never got fixed since.
Now that you mention, I'm pretty sure I've heard Master Edge make mention of the sort.
Well, nothing to do about it now, such as it is.
Don't you... feel as though it was a poor decision to build 'The Great Aquapit?'
What for? It's an outcome we're willing to take.
True, that. We've never rejected any of the naysay from Master Edge.
He warned as much when we decided to build this 'Great Aquapit' instead of repairing that hole in the dome.
And we only made that decision once we understood and accepted the potential danger.
All it is now is taking responsibility for the outcome of our choice.
So it is, surface-dweller. You don't need to give us any pity.
Think about it this way: we've spent a good long summer at least, haven't we?
I completely agree.
Though, evacuating, eh... Haven't decided what I'm going to leave behind. Have you?
I'm nothing like you are. If you ask me right now, my house here's my most wanted and most satisfying design yet.
That's fair. I hope that Originium vein's not going off too easily. I still need to think a little.
How about we go for a drink for now? Some Old Karl Mead?
Not my poison. I'll rather be having a No. 7 Whiskey.
I'd taken Durins to all be buffoons barely smarter than the Archosauria above them... It seems that was hardly the case.
*sigh*...
What's the outlook, Master Edge?
We'll need a little more time to be sure, but based on my experience—
It'll go off within a month.
A month?
Then we should lay the railway while we have the chance, and arrange to evacuate the residents through the tunnel.
Not a chance. Not enough time.
Much more feasible if we dig a temporary shelter deeper in.
We're all aware the dome might be able to suppress some of the vein's explosive force, should it go off.
But the real issue is the Originium dust scattering afterwards!
Once it happens, that dust will fill this entire space, and all of us will be affected by it!
We have to repair the railway despite any danger at this point, and then we clear any obstructions and have everyone take refuge in Ortzimuga!
But the tunnel's already wall to wall with Originium now! It'll be a nightmare to pass through safely!
Zeruertza's home to hundreds of thousands! Do you not understand how much time it'll take to feed them carefully through that tunnel? It's two by two at most in there, you realize that?!
Hey... cool the quibbling.
Gavial, I'll get to you in a moment. Master Edge, do you honestly have a better plan?
I believe we should begin immediately digging away from the Originium vein.
We dig a small-scale shelter, big enough to hold everyone, then we seal off the way to it. It's just a question of digging deep enough...
It'll be a harrowing feat to construct a shelter for hundreds of thousands in this short a time, even with all the technology Zeruertza has.
And besides, that's hundreds of thousands of lives to support! What do you propose there?!
And you think waltzing through the tunnel solves that?!
At least there'll be leeway to begin to do so, provided we pass the most dangerous part of the vein!
But if we stay in a temporary shelter, then consider what happens once the Originium seals off our singular passage to any other city.
We'll have nothing to look forward to but a drawn-out death!
Ugh, I can see your point there, but I still believe...
Of course I understand the risk in my argument! But it's the least worst option we have now!
Y'know... Avdotya.
If it's not important, can you hold your peace for now, Gavial?
Don't really want to disturb you guys either, but—
Seeing as the tunnel's blocked...
Our only option left is to fix that Super Convenient Lift-o-Matic #1 Tomimi mentioned, bring all the Durins to the surface and let 'em live with our tribes for a while, right?
I mean, anything wrong with that idea? You guys have been arguing over this forever.
We head for the... surface?
Yeah.
Uh, guys... did you never consider that before?
Head for the surface?
The Durins have never been to the surface before!
Uh, didn't Stitch? And the people who brought all those library books back from overground?
And look, nothing in the rulebook says you can't, right?
I mean, listen to you guys. The tunnel's too dangerous, a new shelter's gonna take too long.
So, you head up top and take refuge in Acahualla for a while. That's the best plan to me.
That lift could hardly hold so many...
What?
I really want to refute you, but...
Your group missed it during your time in Zeruertza, but trains are by far the most common means of transport in Durin society.
Though lifts are convenient for vertical movement...
Durin cities aren't nomadic, so they face the risk of destruction from the moment they're built. Lifts that can only move vertically have far less use to them than trains do.
Moreover, Durin explorers have calculated that Durin cities often lie 1000 to 1500m below the surface. Hardly any cities exist outside of that range.
Which is to say, Durins probe the underground in horizontals. It's rare for there to be one city just a hundred, maybe two hundred meters below another.
Uh... so what does this have to do with the lift?
I know explaining this to you is a waste of time, but I have to show responsibility for others here.
What? All you're trying to tell me is trains are more Durin-handy, yeah?
*sigh* I suppose so.
And given that, what purpose would a lift then have for existing?
I get it. It's for resources, isn't it?
I don't claim to know why the Durins tend to expand their cities horizontally. Some kind of ingrained habit, I guess.
But forget that. It doesn't mean they scout out resources horizontally as well. Right?
I thought you were playing the braggart when you claimed you'd been places. I can definitely see your prowess now.
Wowie.
Gavial, check it. Even Miss Avdotya here sees my worth better than you do.
Big whoop. Don't care.
Miss Avdotya, next time you get into an argument with Gavial, I'm on your side.
I'll be fine, thank you.
Anyhow, you're correct. The Durins are filled with a passion for probing underground, especially for minerals.
In addition, city planning requires the joint decisions of many, while digging is something each Durin is free to commit to at their leisure.
Consequently, they often end up digging several hundred meters above or below their cities out of sheer unawareness.
In fact, quite a number of Durin historians believe...
The first Durin lift was only designed when an industrial representative discovered a vein in the roof of their very own city, and that it would be most convenient to dig straight to it.
However, following an unchecked surge of Durin interest in the surface, such lifts have often been used as shortcuts to the overground.
Huh... so you're saying Durins mainly use it to shift ore, and sometimes there's little scamps like Stitch hitching a ride on them to have some surface fun.
I've told you, I'm about the same age as you, Gavial!
Uh-huh, sure. So, I mean, I get all that, but I still don't understand what you're getting AT.
*sigh*...
Miss Avdotya's trying to say that since Durin lifts are mostly designed for industry, they're real sturdy stuff.
Tomimi and me sure noticed that when we came down. The lift could hold a few dozen people, no sweat.
So, Gavial, your suggestion's pretty feasible.
Right, Master Edge?
Hear, hear.
I can confirm, the Super Convenient Lift-o-Matic #1 was designed after a vote, which came after some numbskull with nothing to do found a vein up above.
But then we found a vein even closer, so the thing went by the wayside.
Oh, that tracks. I heard that one cave got mined clean. You think it was you Durin guys who dug the entire cave out in the first place?
That, I couldn't say. It's possible.
In addition, to cope with potential future situations, the Durins would have naturally dug out more space while planning the lift, for ease of future enlargement.
Which means, if we're going to make the most reasonable use of our remaining time, enlarging the lifts and getting the Durins to the surface is definitely the way to go.
Oh. You could've just said that then.
If you only know what to do, and not why you're doing it, then you'll run into setbacks someday.
Yeah, I know that.
But I'm a doctor, Avdotya.
And look, you know it deep down, don't you?
How could I ever not trust you?
Why didn't anyone tell me we were getting along so well?
In my opinion, we're already best buds.
Long and short of it is, I approve of Gavial's suggestion.
You can hammer out the remaining details. I'll be continuing the survey.
I'll help you out, Master Edge. My Arts can transmit info. If you turn up any results, I can notify everyone first thing.
Aye.
Oh, yeah, I forgot.
Miss Avdotya, I understand. You're against returning to the surface, and your own experiences inform that.
But you're one of Zeruertza's driving citizens, too. Your opinion swings things.
And you're an honorable person, on top of that. Now's not the time for it, and you get that, don't you?
...*sigh*.
Alright, everyone. Rustle us up a happy plan.
I'll help Master Edge too. No point in tossing around landmark designs now.
Huh? Not like there's anything you can do in there, right?
Can I not be concerned for my own city?
Oh. Sure. Knock yourself out.
So, Avdotya, you still against it?
(Deep breath in and out)
If this is the best method we have to save the people of Zeruertza, then my small contrarianism hardly amounts to anything.
However. Gavial.
You're very strong. Stronger, even, than most Ursus soldiers I've met.
But this isn't a problem you can solve with just strength.
Have you thought about how we're dealing with hundreds of thousands of Durins here?
If hundreds of thousands of people showed up out of nowhere in a region of Sargon, do you know how terrifying that would be?
This isn't something you can solve just by telling us your rainforest is big enough.
How will the region's lord ameer react?
And how will Sargon as a nation react?
They're going to the surface because they believe in you, but if the reality awaiting them is crueler still...
Do you still have the courage to say that no other way will work?
Gavial, you would be the most fitting lord ameer for Acahualla. You are the only one who can rein in each and every Tiacauh.
Everyone is awaiting your return.
And if you simply cannot do it, then it's ultimately likely... that a lord ameer from the Court of Sargon will come to govern Acahualla instead.
And when that happens, Acahualla will no longer be the free land you knew.
Gavial swallows a sliver of hesitation.
She realizes the choice is now facing her, waiting, and she didn't even notice that it came.
No, she's not unwilling to assume this duty. Far from it.
Not even on her vagrant trails, not even in her days on end at Rhodes Island, did she ever forget Acahualla and her people.
However—
Neither has she forgotten the decision she made, when she resolved to leave Acahualla in the first place.
She was going to study medicine. She was going to save the Oripathy sufferers.
Those two choices she made were the right ones, and they were what she wanted. That's how simple it was. That's why she's never found it this hard to choose.
So it seems it's crossed your mind. You're not that much a simpleton.
So, Gavial. If you truly can settle this problem, then I would be willing to even persuade the Durins for you.
What is your answer?
But. Just as Avdotya says, if this is the only way to save Zeruertza...
If her becoming the lord ameer would solve this problem...
If this is the price she must pay to abide by her own ideals—
Then in that case—
I—
I—I'll be Acahualla's lord ameer, and I'll bear the responsibility for this!
Tomimi?!
Zeruertza's... going to be destroyed?
Ha, haha... that's a seriously unfunny joke.
Stitch lifts his head, and lifts his gaze to the dome.
He'd been avoiding repairing it all along. Avoiding redesigning the thing, perhaps.
He'd always assumed he'd still have time.
Only now does he realize, he's out. It's gone. He has time no longer.
Teacher, it seems my hope of surpassing you is behind me.
Stitch jumps aboard a small boat, and sails it for the other shore.
He has to find somewhere to calm down.
Tomimi, what are you doing here?
We were bringing Master Edge's equipment and workers over, and came to see how it was going while were here.
Looks like we were right to do it.
You two as well... Hold on, so, Tomimi, have you ever actually, seriously, considered what you just said?
Of course I have!
Acahualla's always been without a lord ameer, Miss Avdotya.
It's just like you said. If all those Durins went to the surface, they'd draw the attention of the Sargon government for sure.
So before that happens, I—I'll be the Sargon lord ameer to govern the Acahualla region, and I'll make all of this smooth and simple!
No lord ameer? Even then, becoming a lord ameer isn't...
I hold the status of Sargon's government-posted Acahuallan Messenger... and scout.
If I assist, then provided Acahualla comes to have a lord ameer before drawing government attention, this incident can be wholly suppressed.
That should ease some of your concern, correct?
But, Tomimi, you...
It's alright! This way, you don't need to go to any trouble, Gavial!
I mean, yeah, but...
No pressure, Gavial.
Tomimi's volunteering to do it, and if she doesn't, then I can do it too.
Mm-hm!
Zumama...
Or are you scared? You think you made a mistake?
I don't, I just have this weird feeling...
Let me think up a way to describe it.
Yeah. Okay, it's like. I've got this giant boulder in front of me right now.
And I'm thinking, I have to use up so much of my strength just to break it.
But once I give it a shot, something tells me, wow, you can break this thing so easily.
But it's like, why? Even though it's so big.
Gavial, how's the axe I made you? Handy?
Yeah. Honestly, even more handy than I thought. I like it a lot.
I stayed for days straight making it for you, and the materials are all the best I could get too.
What? C'mon, you should've said so sooner. I'm embarrassing myself here.
I'm telling you because I want you to understand—I don't need you to thank me for making these things for you.
Because—
Because Gavial's Gavial!
What the hell does that mean?
Something very simple—
Your fists are bigger than you think, Gavial.
Because you have me.
And me!
And the Rhodes Islanders, and all the people you've saved, and everyone in Acahualla too.
Because in the end, you're right, Gavial.
Ultimately, all those guys you beat into submission are willing to recognize you, because they realize that in the end, you're right.
Gavial, you believe in the right things. You've never gone wrong.
We all believe in you, Gavial.
So, our fists are your fists.
And you can keep on believing in your fists.
All you've gotta do is charge ahead, Gavial! We'll help you out!
You guys...
Oh, but, but—
Zumama, next time you make equipment for Gavial, I want to help too!
I can do it myself.
No, it's unfair only having a Zumama charm! I want to draw on my... my dreamcatcher! And the 'Gavial's Will' mark! And, and...
Uh, no can do, Tomimi, that's too much.

*wail* I'll only draw three!
If you don't let me draw anything, your tail will turn thick!
No putting curses on my tail!
*whimper* Gavial, please don't bonk my head...
Standing by Avdotya, Inam can sense that this Lupo woman to her side is having a somewhat hard time believing her eyes.
But to herself, it's a trivial, banal thing.
She lets out a small sigh.
She knows that finally, she's come to a decision too.
And on that note, I have a better proposal.
Inam?
I'll serve as Acahualla's lord ameer.
Huh?
I've hung around with you as a government Messenger for all these years. I have the right to stand for lord ameer.
I just didn't want to before, that's all.
Seeing as we'd have to force Tomimi back from Rhodes Island, whereas I've been in Acahualla the whole time, I'd make a tidier fit for lord ameer, wouldn't I?
And I imagine that better assuages your concerns, Avdotya.
Yes, it does, but I have to say, you people really boggle my mind...
They're... no, we're just a couple of fools living in the rainforest.
Inam, are you really sure about this?
Well, this way, you won't need to leave Gavial, will you?
Gavial, I have this feeling we'll be fine friends, won't we?
Hahaha, of course we will!
If you insist, then hell, go right on ahead!
Now I see. All else aside, you've definitely persuaded me. I'll make good on my commitment.
However, Gavial, don't get carried away yet.
An entire city's worth of people are headed for the surface. There's no precedent for something like this in all of Durin history.
It's something the entire city has to decide on, and if the people ultimately dissent, then we're out of luck.
Which is exactly why we need you to talk 'em over.
Not a worry. I made you my promise. I'll do all I can.
Haha. Thanks.
(Under breath) Honestly, I should be thanking you on behalf of all of Zeruertza.
Huh? What did you say?
I said, I need to return and make some preparations.
Alright, I'll—
Are you able to draft my speech for me?
Yeah, no.
Then I suggest you do something that plays to your strengths. If I do need your help at any point, I'll let you know.
Gotcha.
Alright, Inam, we've gotta get you and Zumama back to the surface stat. Evac the elderly and children to someplace around Acahualla.
Next—
Taskforce with the working adults to enlarge the lifts, right?
You got it.
No problem.
What about me, Gavial?
You stay by my side, Tomimi.
Avdotya's good on assistance, so let's go see if we can help Master Edge out with anything.
Mm-hm!
Alright, 'scuse us.
Come to think of it... Zumama.
I know this isn't too realistic, but... I'm supposing we can't return the way you and Gavial originally came?
Yep, that's not quite happening.
Then we'll need someone to show the way. The cave network's considerably complex above the network. I haven't forgotten as much for a single second.
Stitch could work, since he brought us down. Elysium too; he might even find the way better than Stitch did.
No need. Seeing as we still have time, you come with me.
Huh?
I'll be honest; luck played an enormous part in Stitch emerging from such a complex network alive.
And Elysium's helping Master Edge out, so let's let him be here.
You mean to say you know the way?
The likelihood is, nobody in modern-day Zeruertza does.

But you know the Durin adventurers, filled with the spirit of exploration? Each time they finish a foray, they're always happy to record everything they've seen and heard in books, so they can share with their companions.
And in the library, I once read such an adventurer's log, which finely detailed how he went through the cave network above the lift, and made it to the surface.
And while I don't know where said adventurer is now, he was considerate enough to leave behind a map.
You know, Master Edge, I'm kind of intrigued. What was Stitch's mentor like?
I just knew you had an ulterior motive for coming with me, you runt.
You bother yourself with that Stitch boy an awful lot.
Hahah, you caught me red-handed.
That's just the kind of guy I am, hoping for everyone to hit it off and make friends.
Then you'd love his mentor.
Finch Canvas was his name. Most well-loved architectural designer in all of Zeruertza.
His style might've been leagues apart from the Durin vogue, but when he explained things, he always our hearts doing it.
